title: Alain Mercier
text:
# Alain Mercier

## Basic Information

**Full Name:** Alain Étienne Mercier
**Species:** Human (Solomani)
**Citizenship:** Solomani Confederation (Jardin)
**Birth Year:** 1061
**Birthplace:** Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin
**Occupation:** Minister of Genetics, Government of Jardin
**Political Affiliation:** Solomani Party (Technocratic Reform Bloc)
**Status:** Deceased (1105)

---

## Overview

Alain Mercier was a Jardinese geneticist, civil servant, and politician who served as Minister of Genetics during the administration of Supreme President Leo Duprey. Widely regarded as one of the foremost experts on population genetics and demographic analysis within the Solomani Confederation, Mercier oversaw the Ministry of Genetics during a period of unprecedented expansion in genetic research and data collection.

Although generally viewed as a loyal supporter of the Solomani Cause, Mercier developed a reputation for placing scientific findings above political convenience. His insistence on evidence-based policy earned him respect among researchers and academics but often placed him at odds with powerful political interests.

Mercier became a figure of subsector-wide interest shortly before his death when he announced plans to release the results of a major long-term demographic study at the upcoming Solomani Party Congress.

---

## Early Life

Born in the capital city of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Mercier was the son of two senior administrators employed by Jardin's Eugenics Bureau. Identified at an early age as possessing exceptional intellectual aptitude, he was selected for advanced educational programs and graduated with distinction from the Jardin Institute of Biological Sciences.

His academic work focused on long-term population stability, hereditary trait distribution, and the interaction between social planning and genetic outcomes. Several of his early papers became required reading within Confederation genetics programs.

---

## Academic Career

Before entering government service, Mercier spent nearly two decades as a researcher and lecturer. He gained prominence through his work analyzing the effects of large-scale demographic interventions across multiple generations.

His findings challenged several accepted assumptions regarding population management and brought him to the attention of senior Party officials. Unlike many researchers who remained within academia, Mercier demonstrated an unusual ability to communicate complex scientific findings to political leaders.

By his mid-forties he had become one of the Confederation's most influential voices on genetics policy.

---

## Minister of Genetics

Mercier was appointed Minister of Genetics in 1097 following the retirement of his predecessor.

During his tenure the Ministry expanded several major initiatives, including:

* Confederation-wide genetic archive modernization.
* Longitudinal population studies.
* Enhanced hereditary disease screening.
* Interplanetary demographic monitoring.
* Research into population resilience and long-term genetic stability.

Although publicly supportive of existing policy, Mercier repeatedly argued that no social system could remain effective unless it was subjected to continual scientific review.

His ministry became known for its unusually rigorous standards and independence from factional politics.

---

## Political Views

Mercier was often described as a technocrat rather than an ideologue.

While loyal to Jardin and the Solomani Cause, he frequently emphasized that scientific institutions should not be influenced by political considerations.

His most famous public statement was delivered shortly before his death:

> "We owe it to the citizens of Jardin to follow the data wherever it leads. The strength of our society has always been built upon scientific rigor, honest evaluation, and the courage to improve."

Supporters viewed this philosophy as evidence of intellectual integrity.

Critics argued that it demonstrated political naivety.

---

## Final Months

In the final year of his life, Mercier directed a comprehensive review of demographic and genetic data collected over multiple generations.

At a press conference held at the Hall of Genetics, he announced that his ministry would reveal significant findings at the upcoming Solomani Party Congress.

The announcement generated intense speculation throughout political, military, and academic circles.

Observers noted that Mercier's remarks were unusually direct and appeared to suggest conclusions with potentially far-reaching implications.

## Session 1 - Assassination

During [[Session 1 - Official Business]], the characters saw a news broadcast reporting that Mercier would reveal corruption in the genetic-affairs files at the coming Party Congress. The broadcast framed the announcement as potentially revolutionary for the government.

Shortly afterward, Mercier rushed through the office area at the [[Hall des Futurs Choisis]] and was killed by multiple shots. The scene appeared designed to make the player characters look responsible: several characters detected signs that their own weapons had moved, fired, or been made to appear as if they fired.

Items recovered from Mercier:

- [[Mercier's Locked Pad]]
- His phone.
- Concealed armor, which failed to stop the barrage.

[[Marcel Delphine]] later claimed Mercier's real discovery pointed to corruption "from above," not to the [[Jardin Syndicate]]'s genetic identity manipulation.

---

## Press Materials

### Hall of Genetics News Capture

![Fraud at the Hall of Genetics? Minister Mercier promises major findings at upcoming Party Congress.](/wiki/media/Alain-Mercier-Hall-of-Genetics-News.png)

### Minister Mercier Announcement Audio

<audio controls src="/wiki/media/Alain-Mercier-Party-Congress-Announcement.mp3">
  Minister Mercier announcement audio.
</audio>

---

## Death

On 143-1105, while attending official business at the Hall of Genetics in Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Mercier was assassinated.

The murder shocked Jardin and rapidly became one of the most significant criminal and political investigations in recent Confederation history.

His death occurred only weeks before the scheduled release of his ministry's report.

The contents of the report have never been publicly disclosed.

---

## Legacy

Alain Mercier remains a controversial figure in Jardinese history.

To supporters, he embodied the principle that scientific truth should guide public policy regardless of political consequences.

To critics, he represented a dangerous willingness to question institutions that had maintained stability for centuries.

His unfinished work continues to be the subject of speculation among historians, political analysts, and genetic researchers throughout the Solomani Confederation.

title: Arcosphere Rousseau
text:
# Arcosphère Rousseau

## Rousseau Arcology

**Location:** Central Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin

**Type:** Urban Arcology, Residential Megastructure, Commercial Hub

**Administrative Authority:** Arcology Administration Directorate

**Governor:** Adrien Pelletier

**Population:** 1.8 Billion Permanent Residents

**Daily Population:** Approximately 2.3 Billion

**Public Access:** Open

**Nickname:** *"Rousseau"*

---

# Overview

The **Arcosphère Rousseau** (Rousseau Arcology) is the largest structure on Jardin and one of the most densely populated urban environments in the Solomani Confederation.

Dominating the center of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, the arcology forms a massive crescent surrounding the Grand Axis and serves as the residential, commercial, industrial, and cultural heart of the planetary capital.

To outsiders, Rousseau appears impossible.

To residents, it is simply home.

Nearly one in seven citizens of Jardin lives within its walls.

Entire generations are born, educated, employed, married, and buried without ever leaving the arcology.

Government planners proudly describe Rousseau as:

> "Human civilization perfected."

Residents typically describe it as:

> "Crowded."

---

# History

## The Arcology Initiative

Construction began nearly two centuries ago during the Great Expansion.

Rapid population growth threatened to consume valuable agricultural land surrounding Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

Rather than expand outward indefinitely, planners chose a different solution.

The city would expand upward.

The Arcology Initiative combined:

* Housing
* Industry
* Transportation
* Commerce
* Education
* Recreation

into a single integrated structure.

The project became one of the largest construction efforts in Confederation history.

---

## The Rousseau Doctrine

Named after the ancient Terran philosopher Jean-Jacques Rousseau, the arcology was intended to demonstrate that careful planning could create a superior urban environment.

Government publications described it as:

> "A city designed for citizens rather than automobiles."

The original plans envisioned housing approximately 800 million residents.

Reality proved more complicated.

---

## The Quiet Expansion

Over successive generations, unused maintenance spaces, storage sections, and service corridors were gradually converted into residential and commercial districts.

Some expansions were legal.

Others were not.

The result is a structure far larger than official plans ever intended.

No one knows its true size.

---

# Architecture

From above, Rousseau resembles an immense crescent embracing the Grand Axis of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

The structure rises hundreds of meters above the city and extends deep beneath ground level.

Major features include:

* Residential towers
* Commercial districts
* Internal parks
* Transit networks
* Schools
* Hospitals
* Manufacturing centers

The arcology contains enough internal infrastructure to function independently for months if isolated.

---

# Major Districts

## Les Sommets

### The Summit Levels

The highest and most prestigious districts.

Home to:

* Senior Party officials
* Corporate executives
* Leading scientists
* Wealthy professionals

Features include:

* Private sky gardens
* Luxury apartments
* Exclusive clubs
* Fine dining establishments

Many apartments possess direct views of the Palace of Continuity.

Residents insist this is merely coincidence.

---

## Les Niveaux Civiques

### Civic Levels

The professional middle class.

Home to:

* Teachers
* Engineers
* Doctors
* Administrators
* Researchers

These districts represent the idealized vision of Jardin society.

Clean.

Efficient.

Safe.

Almost every government promotional video is filmed here.

---

## Les Communes

### Commons Levels

The largest section of Rousseau.

Home to more than a billion residents.

Contains:

* Housing blocks
* Schools
* Markets
* Restaurants
* Recreation facilities

This is where most ordinary citizens live.

Visitors often compare it to a city existing entirely indoors.

---

## Les Ateliers

### Industrial Levels

The manufacturing heart of the arcology.

Contains:

* Factories
* Warehouses
* Robot maintenance facilities
* Recycling systems
* Cargo handling centers

Thousands of freight vehicles move through the district every hour.

Most visitors never see it.

Most residents depend upon it.

---

## Les Profondeurs

### The Depths

The lowest officially recognized districts.

Contains:

* Utility systems
* Transit infrastructure
* Water processing facilities
* Power distribution networks

Officially sparsely populated.

Unofficially not.

---

# The Unofficial City

## Les Ombres

### The Shadows

The name given to unregistered districts hidden within the arcology.

These areas do not officially exist.

Generations of unauthorized construction have created:

* Informal neighborhoods
* Hidden marketplaces
* Illegal workshops
* Refugee communities
* Smuggling routes

Authorities periodically deny their existence.

Everyone else knows better.

---

# Daily Life

Life inside Rousseau rarely requires leaving the structure.

Residents have access to:

* Employment
* Education
* Entertainment
* Healthcare
* Transportation

within walking distance.

Many citizens spend months without seeing open sky.

Artificial lighting systems simulate natural day-night cycles.

Most residents stop noticing.

---

# Transportation

Rousseau possesses one of the most advanced transit systems in the Confederation.

Transportation methods include:

* Maglev trains
* Moving pedestrian corridors
* Autonomous shuttles
* Freight elevators
* Grav lifts

The average resident can reach any major district within thirty minutes.

---

# Commerce

The arcology contains millions of businesses.

Everything from:

* Street vendors
* Family restaurants
* Boutique manufacturers
* Corporate headquarters

can be found within Rousseau.

Many of the Confederation's largest corporations maintain offices here.

---

# Culture

Rousseau possesses its own distinct identity.

Residents often describe themselves as:

> "Rousseans first, Jardinais second."

Whether this is intended as a joke remains unclear.

The arcology has developed:

* Unique slang
* Local traditions
* Distinct cuisine
* Neighborhood rivalries

Many citizens never fully identify with the broader city.

---

# Major Landmarks

## Forum Rousseau

The central public square.

Location of:

* Public speeches
* Demonstrations
* Cultural festivals

The largest indoor gathering space on Jardin.

---

## Market of a Thousand Worlds

A sprawling commercial district.

Contains goods from across the Confederation and beyond.

One of the best places on Jardin to find something unusual.

---

## The People's Conservatory

A vast indoor park.

Contains:

* Lakes
* Forests
* Walking paths

Many residents visit simply to experience something resembling nature.

---

## The Arcology Museum

Documents the history of Rousseau and urban development throughout the Confederation.

Popular with school groups.

Not popular with schoolchildren.

---

# Security

Public security is maintained by:

* Arcology Security Directorate
* Municipal Police
* SolSec liaison officers

Security levels vary considerably between districts.

The Summit Levels are among the safest places on Jardin.

The Shadows are not.

---

# Criminal Activity

Despite government claims, Rousseau possesses a substantial underworld.

Activities include:

* Smuggling
* Identity fraud
* Unregistered robotics
* Counterfeiting
* Information theft

Most criminal activity occurs within The Shadows.

---

# The Jardin Syndicate

The arcology serves as the primary operational center of the Jardin Syndicate.

Officially this is false.

Everyone knows it is true.

Numerous front organizations operate within the Commons and Industrial districts.

Their influence is particularly strong among:

* Migrant populations
* Refugee communities
* Unregistered residents

The Syndicate's unofficial slogan is frequently heard in the Shadows:

> "Everything is possible. It simply isn't approved."

---

# Notable Individuals

## Adrien Pelletier

**Governor of Rousseau**

A dedicated administrator who genuinely believes the arcology represents humanity's future.

---

## Nadia Giroux

**Investigative Journalist**

Known for exposing corruption and embarrassing powerful individuals.

Frequently receives anonymous threats.

Frequently ignores them.

---

## Sister Celeste Moreau

**Community Organizer**

Operates charitable programs throughout the lower districts.

Widely respected by residents.

---

## Jacques Martin

**Business Executive**

Officially owns several logistics companies.

Unofficially rumored to maintain extensive connections within the Jardin Syndicate.

---

# Public Opinion

Government officials point to Rousseau as proof of the success of Solomani planning.

Residents generally agree.

They simply disagree about why.

---

# Rumors

## The Lost Levels

Entire districts sealed during construction still exist beneath the arcology.

---

## The Hidden Registry

An illegal copy of citizen records exists somewhere within The Shadows.

---

## The Shepherd's Court

Marcel Delphine allegedly maintains a hidden residence somewhere inside Rousseau.

No one agrees where.

---

## The Ghost Transit Line

A maglev route appears on no official maps but continues to operate.

Passengers report reaching destinations that should not exist.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Citizen

A resident vanishes from every government database.

The only lead points toward the Shadows.

### Arcology Election

A local Party Congress threatens to expose major corruption.

Several factions seek outside assistance.

### Ghost District

Maintenance crews discover a forgotten neighborhood sealed for decades.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

The Travellers receive an invitation to meet Marcel Delphine.

The location changes every time it is transmitted.

### The Registry War

The Hall of Chosen Futures and the Jardin Syndicate begin a quiet conflict over stolen citizen records.

The Travellers become caught in the middle.

### Lost Beneath Rousseau

A child disappears into the unregistered sections of the arcology.

Finding them requires entering places that officially do not exist.

## Session 1 Notes

The player characters live in the Rousseau arcology, though on different floors, classes, elevators, and routines. After the [[Mercier Assassination]], a driverless grav speeder returned toward their home but used waste-disposal access rather than a public entrance.

The vehicle entered hidden service routes beneath the arcology. [[Victor Mendes (Steinbach)]] recognized that criminal organizations on Jardin are rumored to use access ways, cleaning-material locations, venting paths, and similar spaces as headquarters in the cracks of the city.

---
:::gm

# Referee Notes

Rousseau should feel alive.

Unlike the Palace of Continuity, which represents authority, or the Hall of Chosen Futures, which represents administration, Rousseau represents people.

It contains everything.

Success and failure.

Wealth and poverty.

Order and chaos.

Idealism and corruption.

If the Palace is the mind of Jardin and the Hall is its nervous system, then Rousseau is its beating heart.

Most campaigns set on Jardin will eventually spend more time here than anywhere else.

The arcology is large enough to contain an entire campaign by itself.


:::gm
GM-only truth goes here.
:::

Passengers report reaching destinations that should not exist.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Citizen

A resident vanishes from every government database.

The only lead points toward the Shadows.

### Arcology Election

A local Party Congress threatens to expose major corruption.

Several factions seek outside assistance.

### Ghost District

Maintenance crews discover a forgotten neighborhood sealed for decades.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

The Travellers receive an invitation to meet Marcel Delphine.

The location changes every time it is transmitted.

### The Registry War

The Hall of Chosen Futures and the Jardin Syndicate begin a quiet conflict over stolen citizen records.

The Travellers become caught in the middle.

### Lost Beneath Rousseau

A child disappears into the unregistered sections of the arcology.

Finding them requires entering places that officially do not exist.

## Session 1 Notes

The player characters live in the Rousseau arcology, though on different floors, classes, elevators, and routines. After the [[Mercier Assassination]], a driverless grav speeder returned toward their home but used waste-disposal access rather than a public entrance.

The vehicle entered hidden service routes beneath the arcology. [[Victor Mendes (Steinbach)]] recognized that criminal organizations on Jardin are rumored to use access ways, cleaning-material locations, venting paths, and similar spaces as headquarters in the cracks of the city.

---
gm
GM-only truth goes here.
:::

title: Carlos
summary: Old Navy acquaintance associated with a poker game on the Venture; possibly connected to future jump contacts.
text:
# Carlos

## Overview

Carlos is an old shipboard acquaintance mentioned when [[Cass Pien (Kolb)]] and another veteran recognized each other from service aboard the Venture. He used to run a poker game and is remembered as a cheat and poor bluffer, though still "loyal to the party" in the joking recollection.

Near the end of the session, the GM mentions "Carlos contacts" or "Carlos Contax" in connection with moving the group through the next jump once they are off Jardin. The exact name and role need human review.

## Appears In

- [[Session 1 - Official Business]]

## Notes

- Associated with prior Navy service and poker.
- May be relevant to off-world movement after the group escapes Jardin.

title: Crèche District 14
text:
# Crèche District 14

*"Every Child, A Future."*

**Location:** Eastern Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

**Type:** State Child Development Arcology

**Administrative Authority:** Directorate of Child Development

**Status:** Active

**Population:** 1.2 Million Children

**Staff:** 180,000 Educators, Medical Personnel, and Administrators

**Nickname:** District 14

---

# Overview

Crèche District 14 is one of the largest child development complexes on Jardin.

It serves as home, school, community, and developmental center for over a million children from infancy through adolescence.

Unlike most societies, children in District 14 are not raised by biological parents.

They are raised by the State.

To most Jardinese citizens, this is entirely normal.

To foreign visitors, it is often deeply unsettling.

---

# First Impressions

Visitors expecting a grim institution are often surprised.

District 14 is beautiful.

Clean.

Quiet.

Orderly.

Safe.

Children play in parks.

Students attend classes.

Sports teams practice in open fields.

Music drifts from performance halls.

Everything appears healthy and happy.

That is what makes visitors uncomfortable.

Nothing appears wrong.

---

# Philosophy

District 14 operates under a simple belief:

> No child should be limited by the circumstances of birth.

Every child receives:

- Housing
- Nutrition
- Medical care
- Education
- Psychological support
- Recreational opportunities

The system does not view itself as replacing parents.

It views itself as replacing inequality.

---

# Structure

Children are organized into developmental cohorts called:

## Maisons

Houses.

Each Maison contains approximately:

- 120 Children
- 12 Mentors
- 8 Educational Staff
- Medical Support Personnel

Children remain with the same Maison for years.

The House becomes their family.

---

# Mentors

The closest equivalent to parents.

Mentors are highly trained professionals responsible for:

- Emotional development
- Discipline
- Guidance
- Education support

Many remain with the same cohort for decades.

Former students frequently maintain lifelong relationships with mentors.

---

# Education

District 14 contains:

- Primary Schools
- Secondary Schools
- Technical Academies
- Arts Programs
- Athletic Institutes

Educational outcomes are among the highest in the Confederation.

The district produces:

- Scientists
- Administrators
- Military Officers
- Engineers
- Artists

at remarkable rates.

---

# Daily Life

Children live structured lives.

A typical day includes:

- Education
- Physical Exercise
- Community Service
- Social Development
- Recreation

Schedules are carefully balanced.

The goal is not obedience.

The goal is optimization.

---

# The Memory Gardens

The most unusual area of District 14.

Every child possesses a personal record archive.

Messages from biological parents.

Medical records.

Academic achievements.

Personal milestones.

Upon adulthood citizens gain access to these records.

For many, this is the first time they learn much about their biological origins.

---

# Relationship with Biological Parents

Biological parents are not secret.

Nor are they central.

Most children know:

- Their genetic contributors
- Their heritage
- Their family history

But these relationships are considered secondary.

The Maison is primary.

---

# Public Opinion

Most Jardinese consider District 14 a success.

Supporters point to:

- Low crime
- Excellent education
- Social equality
- High productivity

Critics argue that something important has been lost.

Most struggle to define exactly what.

---

# Security

District 14 maintains its own branch of Social Solidarity.

Officers are rarely seen.

Problems are usually addressed long before force becomes necessary.

Visitors often describe the district as:

> "The safest place on the planet."

---

# Notable Locations

## Hall of First Steps

Infant development center.

Every citizen of District 14 begins here.

---

## Unity Stadium

Largest youth athletics facility on Jardin.

---

## Garden of Futures

A massive public park where graduation ceremonies occur.

---

## The Reflection Pool

A quiet memorial dedicated to former students.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Director Elise Fournier

Administrator of District 14.

Widely respected.

Known throughout Jardin as:

> "The Mother of a Million."

---

## Mentor Jean Mercier

One of the oldest serving mentors.

Three generations of graduates have passed through his Maison.

---

## Dr. Amelie Rousseau

Chief Developmental Psychologist.

Her research shapes educational policy across Jardin.

---

# What Foreigners Notice

Visitors usually report three things:

1. The children appear remarkably confident.
2. The children appear unusually independent.
3. The children rarely speak about parents.

The third observation is usually the one that stays with them.

---

# Rumors

## The Lost Maison

A House that supposedly disappeared from official records decades ago.

---

## The Original Children

Records of the first generation raised under the Perfection System remain sealed.

---

## The Questionnaires

Every child completes extensive annual assessments.

Conspiracy theorists claim these evaluations serve purposes beyond education.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

District 14 should never feel like an orphanage.

It should feel like a society that genuinely believes it has solved childhood.

Visitors should leave asking themselves:

> "The children seem happy."

And then immediately wondering:

> "So why does this place make me uncomfortable?"


GM-only truth goes here.
:::

Children live structured lives.

A typical day includes:

- Education
- Physical Exercise
- Community Service
- Social Development
- Recreation

Schedules are carefully balanced.

The goal is not obedience.

The goal is optimization.

---

# The Memory Gardens

The most unusual area of District 14.

Every child possesses a personal record archive.

Messages from biological parents.

Medical records.

Academic achievements.

Personal milestones.

Upon adulthood citizens gain access to these records.

For many, this is the first time they learn much about their biological origins.

---

# Relationship with Biological Parents

Biological parents are not secret.

Nor are they central.

Most children know:

- Their genetic contributors
- Their heritage
- Their family history

But these relationships are considered secondary.

The Maison is primary.

---

# Public Opinion

Most Jardinese consider District 14 a success.

Supporters point to:

- Low crime
- Excellent education
- Social equality
- High productivity

Critics argue that something important has been lost.

Most struggle to define exactly what.

---

# Security

District 14 maintains its own branch of Social Solidarity.

Officers are rarely seen.

Problems are usually addressed long before force becomes necessary.

Visitors often describe the district as:

> "The safest place on the planet."

---

# Notable Locations

## Hall of First Steps

Infant development center.

Every citizen of District 14 begins here.

---

## Unity Stadium

Largest youth athletics facility on Jardin.

---

## Garden of Futures

A massive public park where graduation ceremonies occur.

---

## The Reflection Pool

A quiet memorial dedicated to former students.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Director Elise Fournier

Administrator of District 14.

Widely respected.

Known throughout Jardin as:

> "The Mother of a Million."

---

## Mentor Jean Mercier

One of the oldest serving mentors.

Three generations of graduates have passed through his Maison.

---

## Dr. Amelie Rousseau

Chief Developmental Psychologist.

Her research shapes educational policy across Jardin.

---

# What Foreigners Notice

Visitors usually report three things:

1. The children appear remarkably confident.
2. The children appear unusually independent.
3. The children rarely speak about parents.

The third observation is usually the one that stays with them.

---

# Rumors

## The Lost Maison

A House that supposedly disappeared from official records decades ago.

---

## The Original Children

Records of the first generation raised under the Perfection System remain sealed.

---

## The Questionnaires

Every child completes extensive annual assessments.

Conspiracy theorists claim these evaluations serve purposes beyond education.

---

title: Customs & Immigration Checkpoint Harmony
text:
# Customs & Immigration Checkpoint Harmony
# Poste d'Harmonie Frontalière

*"Every Arrival Matters."*

**Location:** Port Sainte-Jeanne Downport

**Type:** Customs and Immigration Processing Complex

**Administrative Authority:** Directorate of Citizen Integrity

**Operational Control:** Ministry of Social Solidarity

**Status:** Active

**Security Classification:** Controlled Access

**Daily Throughput:** 250,000-400,000 Travelers

**Primary Functions:**
- Immigration Processing
- Customs Inspection
- Citizenship Verification
- Biosecurity Screening
- Visitor Registration
- Repatriation Services

**Nickname:** The Gate

---

# Overview

The **Customs & Immigration Checkpoint Harmony** is the primary entry point into Jardin.

Every traveler entering the planet through Port Sainte-Jeanne eventually passes through its halls.

For most visitors the experience is efficient, professional, and surprisingly polite.

For others it is one of the most stressful experiences of their lives.

The checkpoint serves as the intersection between:

- Social Solidarity
- Customs Authority
- SolSec
- Transstar
- The Hall of Chosen Futures

Every traveler is recorded.

Every declaration is logged.

Every arrival becomes part of the system.

As many Jardinese officials proudly state:

> "A secure society begins with knowing who enters it."

---

# First Impressions

The checkpoint resembles a modern airport terminal scaled to impossible proportions.

Visitors encounter:

- Massive arrival halls
- Automated processing systems
- Biometric scanners
- Citizen service kiosks
- Customs inspection zones

The atmosphere is calm.

Almost too calm.

Announcements are soft.

Queues move quickly.

Officers remain polite.

Nobody raises their voice.

Yet every traveler senses they are being observed.

Because they are.

---

# Architecture

The facility was intentionally designed to project confidence rather than intimidation.

Features include:

- White stone architecture
- Indoor gardens
- Natural lighting systems
- Public art displays
- Quiet processing areas

Government planners wanted arrivals to feel welcomed.

The extensive security measures remain largely hidden from view.

---

# Primary Divisions

## Immigration Processing Hall

The largest section of the facility.

Handles:

- Visitor entry permits
- Residency applications
- Work authorizations
- Colonial transfers

Most travelers spend less than thirty minutes here.

Most.

---

## Citizen Services Bureau

Dedicated to Jardinese citizens.

Provides:

- Identity updates
- Travel registrations
- Repatriation services
- Documentation support

Returning citizens are often directed here before being allowed to proceed.

---

## Customs Inspection Complex

Responsible for:

- Cargo inspections
- Contraband detection
- Duty collection
- Import certification

The Customs Bureau possesses a reputation for thoroughness.

Smugglers possess other descriptions.

---

## Biosecurity Division

Protects Jardin from:

- Disease vectors
- Agricultural contamination
- Biological hazards

The division maintains quarantine facilities capable of isolating thousands of travelers.

---

## Visitor Orientation Center

Provides information regarding:

- Local laws
- Social expectations
- Cultural customs
- Restricted activities

Many visitors later admit they should have paid closer attention.

---

# Social Solidarity Presence

The checkpoint falls under the authority of the **Directorate of Citizen Integrity**.

Their responsibilities include:

- Identity verification
- Travel monitoring
- Population records
- Immigration review

Officers are generally courteous.

They also have access to more information than most travelers realize.

---

# SolSec Liaison Office

A permanent SolSec office operates within the checkpoint.

Official responsibilities include:

- Counterintelligence
- Counterterrorism
- Security investigations

Most travelers never interact with SolSec.

Those who do generally remember the experience.

---

# The Arrival Assessment

One of the facility's most unusual procedures.

Visitors are often asked seemingly harmless questions regarding:

- Travel plans
- Employment
- Personal interests
- Educational background

The process feels conversational.

Many travelers later discover the information was far more valuable than they realized.

---

# Processing Categories

## Green Channel

Low-risk travelers.

Fast processing.

Minimal review.

---

## Blue Channel

Routine review.

Additional verification.

Most visitors fall into this category.

---

## Silver Channel

Enhanced screening.

Usually associated with:

- Foreign government officials
- Corporate executives
- Intelligence concerns

---

## Red Channel

Restricted access.

Few travelers ever see it.

Fewer discuss it afterward.

---

# Notable Locations

## Hall of Welcome

The first chamber encountered by arriving visitors.

Contains:

- Information kiosks
- Currency exchanges
- Transportation services

---

## The Registry Atrium

Where identity records are verified and updated.

Connected directly to systems maintained by the Hall of Chosen Futures.

---

## Observation Gallery

An elevated monitoring center overlooking the facility.

Closed to the public.

---

## Quarantine Annex

Biosecurity facility located beneath the main complex.

Most travelers never know it exists.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Director Simone Laurent

**Chief Administrator**

Responsible for overall operations.

Known for exceptional efficiency.

---

## Inspector Gabriel Rousseau

**Senior Customs Officer**

A legend among anti-smuggling investigators.

---

## Commissioner Elise Martin

**Directorate of Citizen Integrity**

Coordinates Social Solidarity operations within the facility.

---

## Agent Luc Deschamps

**SolSec Liaison Officer**

Officially responsible for interagency coordination.

Unofficially responsible for many other things.

---

# Public Reputation

Most Jardinese citizens appreciate the checkpoint.

They view it as:

- Professional
- Efficient
- Necessary

Foreign visitors often describe it differently.

Common observations include:

- Surprisingly polite
- Extremely thorough
- Unsettlingly organized

---

# Rumors

## The Forgotten Traveler

A visitor allegedly entered the checkpoint decades ago and was never seen leaving.

No records exist.

---

## The Hidden Registry

A classified database beneath the facility contains information unavailable even to the Hall of Chosen Futures.

---

## The Watch List

Certain names automatically trigger responses from multiple government agencies.

No one knows how many names are included.

---

## The Silent Room

A secure interview chamber rumored to be jointly operated by Social Solidarity and SolSec.

Officially it does not exist.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing Passenger

A traveler disappears somewhere inside the checkpoint.

### Smuggled Data

A courier attempts to bring sensitive information onto Jardin.

### False Identity

A visitor arrives carrying perfectly forged documents.

### Quarantine Alert

An arriving vessel triggers emergency biosecurity protocols.

### SolSec Request

An agent asks the Travellers to discreetly observe a newly arrived passenger.

### The Red Channel

A routine arrival is unexpectedly redirected into the facility's most restricted processing area.

---

:::gm
# Referee Notes

The Checkpoint should not feel oppressive.

It should feel inevitable.

Nobody shouts.

Nobody threatens.

Nobody waves weapons.

Instead the system quietly knows:

- Who you are.
- Where you came from.
- Why you arrived.
- Where you are going.

The true power of the checkpoint is not force.

It is information.

For most visitors, passing through the Gate is routine.

For those carrying secrets, it can be one of the most dangerous places on Jardin.

GM-only truth goes here.
:::

# Dan Araujo (Josh)

![Dan Araujo portrait](/wiki/media/Dan-Araujo.png)

*Solomani Racial • Confederation Navy: Technical*

**Age:** 34

**Gender:** Male
**UPP:** STR 7, DEX 12, END 8, INT 14, EDU 12, SOC 12
**Hits:** 25
**Armour:** Cloth (TL10), Protection 8
**Cash:** Cr250
**Ship Shares:** 6

## Profile

Dan Araujo is a sharp-minded, well-connected Solomani Confederation citizen who spent four terms in the Confederation Navy technical branch, leaving as a Petty Officer 1st Class. His career emphasizes engineering, investigation, and shipboard technical competence.

## Notable Skills

Engineer (J-drive) 3, Mechanic 2, Investigate 2, Vacc Suit 2, Astrogation 1, Diplomat 1, Electronics (Remote Ops) 1, Engineer (M-drive) 1, Gunner (Screen) 1, Language (Trokh) 1, Leadership 1, Medic 1, Pilot (Spacecraft) 1, Recon 1, Tactics (Naval) 1.

## Connections

## Session 1 - Official Business

- Initially leaned toward compliance with authorities after the [[Mercier Assassination]], trusting that he had done nothing wrong.
- Stayed with the group as the containment broke into violence and escape.
- After reaching the hideout, repaired [[0-31 Saichi Maru (Annette)]] after the walker descent.
- Focused on tangible technical work rather than drawing attention through risky computer searches.

- Rival: Racial Incident

## Equipment & Benefits

Terran Adventurers Society membership, Navy Combat Shotgun, Navy Combat Shotgun Stun Round, Assault Pistol, Cloth (TL10), Portable Computer/4 (TL13), 6 ship shares.

:::gm
## Import Notes

Imported from Foundry actor export `fvtt-Actor-dan-araujo-(josh)-jTiP2IyMiLd8Tpv4.json`.
:::

title: Elena Verona
summary: Social Solidarity commissar who responded to the Mercier assassination scene and survived the group's escape.
text:
# Elena Verona

## Overview

Elena Verona is a commissar of [[Social Solidarity]] who arrived at the [[Hall des Futurs Choisis]] after the [[Mercier Assassination]]. The transcript first presents her over the speakers as "Elena Verona, Commissar of Social Solidarity," though later table speech also calls her Alina. The spelling should be reviewed.

Verona attempted to calm and contain the scene while Home Guard forces surrounded the characters. After Emma opened fire, Verona was wounded, grappled by [[Victor Mendes (Steinbach)]], and left unconscious during the escape.

## Appears In

- [[Session 1 - Official Business]]
- [[Mercier Frame-Up]]

## Equipment and Assets

- Social Solidarity uniform.
- Sidearm.
- Computerized belt identified as a one-use grav belt.
- Radio/earpiece. Its communication channel produced unusual hiss or interference.

## Notes

- Public broadcasts later used Verona as a harmed witness.
- She survived the escape, making her testimony a major risk.
- The group debated killing her but did not do so in the transcript.

# Emma Colbert (Laura)

![Emma Colbert portrait](/wiki/media/Emma-Colbert.jpg)

*Solomani Racial • SolSec: Secret Agent*

**Player:** Laura
**Age:** 38
**Species:** Solomani Racial
**Gender:** Female
**UPP:** STR 7, DEX 10, END 9, INT 14, EDU 15, SOC 10
**Hits:** 24
**Armour:** Protec Suit; Cloth Trench Coat, Protection 10
**Ship Shares:** 2

## Profile

Emma Colbert is a Solomani Confederation citizen who spent five terms in SolSec as a Secret Agent, rising through field and command work. Her sheet emphasizes informant networks, social leverage, intelligence operations, and a wide spread of civilian, military, corporate, criminal, and diplomatic contacts.

## Notable Skills

Broker 2, Carouse 2, Deception 2, Electronics (Comms) 2, Persuade 2, Advocate 1, Electronics (Computers) 1, Electronics (Sensors) 1, Gun Combat (Slug) 1, Gunner (Turret) 1, Medic 1, Pilot (Spacecraft) 1, Science (Psychology) 1, Stealth 1.

## Connections

## Session 1 - Official Business

- Maintained cover during the initial response to the [[Mercier Assassination]].
- Received a critical warning through an attached/cybernetic computer setup that the authorities intended to kill the group.
- Opened fire on a Home Guard guard, triggering the group's active escape.
- Provided or had access to a safe warehouse with shower and stored food where the group could regroup.
- Held or was trusted with [[Marcel Delphine]]'s one-use communicator.
- Contacted [[Sarah Gay's Son]], a smuggler contact, and learned more about [[Marcel Delphine]], the [[Jardin Syndicate]], and the [[Transstar Sector Exchange]] as a potential refuge.
- Retained a recording of the "kill them all" warning/order.

Emma has an extensive SolSec contact network including Captain Anna Pereira, Christine Hernandez, Anita Hussain, Nancy Wang, Anita Singh, Sergey Sun, Captain Irina Lal, Sergeant Andrea Jin, Lieutenant Robert Rodriguez, Pedro He, Secretary Daniel Garcia, Luis Zhang, Laura Yuan, Nainod, Fatima Yu, Mayer Meeks, and a compartmentalized Marc Duncan bureaucracy contact.

## Equipment & Benefits

Protec Suit, Cloth Trench Coat, Officer's Revolver, SolSec Wafer Jack, SC Courier, Body Camera, Radio Jammer, Neural Comm Basic, Satellite Uplink, Mobile Comm Advanced, 2 ship shares.

:::gm
## Import Notes

Imported from Foundry actor export `fvtt-Actor-emma-colbert-(laura)-fAhaECjHXSjH01V1 (2).json`.
:::

title: False News Broadcast
summary: A fabricated broadcast blamed Mercier's murder on genetic outcasts and falsely showed Zoy attacking the minister.
text:
# False News Broadcast

## Overview

Within hours of the [[Mercier Assassination]], public news reported a terrorist attack at the Hall of Continuity and claimed that Minister [[Alain Mercier]] had been killed by genetic "half-breeds." The report showed fake footage of [[Zoy (Damon)]] throwing a grenade at Mercier.

The broadcast also presented [[Elena Verona]] as a harmed witness and claimed the group killed Mercier's bodyguards.

## Confirmed False Elements

- Zoy did not throw a grenade at Mercier in the transcript.
- Mercier was killed by gunfire linked to the frame-up, not by a grenade.
- The story conceals the fact that the group's weapons appeared to be manipulated or spoofed.

## Uses

- Publicly brands the group as terrorists.
- Links the case to genetic purity politics.
- Gives authorities justification to hunt the group and possibly crack down on the [[Jardin Syndicate]] or other genetically disfavored populations.

text:
# Fort Robespierre Naval Annex

*"Vigilance, Duty, Victory."*

**Location:** Southern Coastal Military Zone, Jardin

**Type:** Naval Installation and Military Training Complex

**Administrative Authority:** Confederation Navy

**Operational Control:** Jardin Naval Command

**Status:** Active Military Facility

**Security Classification:** Restricted

**Population:** 850,000 Military Personnel

**Daily Population:** Approximately 1.5 Million

**Primary Functions:**
- Naval Operations
- Marine Training
- Mech Warfare Development
- Orbital Deployment
- Military Logistics
- Fleet Support

**Nickname:** The Fort

---

# Overview

Fort Robespierre Naval Annex is the military heart of Jardin.

While the public sees the Palace of Continuity as the center of political power and the Hall of Chosen Futures as the center of social planning, military officers understand that true power often resides elsewhere.

It resides at Fort Robespierre.

The Annex serves as:

- Headquarters for major naval forces assigned to Jardin
- Training center for Confederation Marines
- Primary mech warfare academy in the subsector
- Strategic logistics hub
- Surface transportation center for orbital naval facilities

Every day thousands of personnel move between the Annex and the massive military stations orbiting above Jardin.

The facility never closes.

Warships must always remain ready.

---

# History

## Origins

Fort Robespierre traces its roots to the period before Solomani intervention.

The original fortress was constructed during Jardin's expansionist era and served as headquarters for military operations against neighboring systems.

Following Jardin's defeat, the facility was seized and reorganized by Confederation authorities.

Rather than dismantle the military infrastructure, the Solomani Party expanded it.

Over time the Fort became one of the most important naval installations in the Solomani Front.

---

## The Rim War Era

During the Solomani Rim War the Annex operated continuously.

The facility trained:

- Marines
- Fleet crews
- Pilots
- Logistics specialists

Entire generations of veterans passed through its gates.

Many monuments throughout the complex commemorate units destroyed during the war.

---

## Modern Role

Today Fort Robespierre acts as both military headquarters and training institution.

The installation's influence extends throughout the subsector.

Many senior naval officers began their careers here.

---

# First Impressions

The Fort feels fundamentally different from the rest of Jardin.

Visitors encounter:

- Precision
- Discipline
- Purpose

Every movement appears organized.

Every building serves a function.

Even the landscaping feels tactical.

This is not a place designed to impress politicians.

It is designed to prepare for war.

---

# Layout

The Annex occupies hundreds of square kilometers of coastline and inland training territory.

Major districts include:

## Naval Command Citadel

The headquarters of military operations on Jardin.

Contains:

- Fleet command centers
- Strategic planning offices
- Intelligence sections
- Communications arrays

This is where regional naval operations are coordinated.

---

## Marine Training Grounds

The largest military training area on Jardin.

Facilities include:

- Urban combat zones
- Live-fire ranges
- Boarding simulators
- Atmospheric assault courses

Thousands of marines train here every year.

---

## Mech Combat Academy

One of the most famous institutions on the planet.

Responsible for:

- Walker operations
- Powered armor tactics
- Combined-arms doctrine
- Urban warfare training

Pilots from throughout the Confederation compete for admission.

Graduates are highly respected.

---

## Vehicle Development Range

Testing area for:

- Tanks
- Walkers
- Combat drones
- Experimental vehicles

New military designs are frequently evaluated here.

Explosions are common.

---

## Orbital Transfer Complex

The busiest military transportation hub on Jardin.

Serves as the primary connection between:

- Surface facilities
- Orbital stations
- Fleet anchorages

Personnel transfer continuously between the Fort and orbit.

Most naval officers spend considerable time here.

---

## Fleet Logistics Center

Handles:

- Munitions
- Spare parts
- Fuel supplies
- Vehicle maintenance

One of the largest military warehouses in the subsector.

---

# Orbital Facilities

The Annex itself does not house major warships.

Those vessels remain in orbit.

Instead the Fort serves as the surface support facility for:

## Robespierre Fleet Anchorage

Massive orbital station supporting:

- Destroyers
- Cruisers
- Fleet auxiliaries
- Assault transports

---

## Marine Orbital Barracks

Housing and deployment center for marine forces.

---

## Strategic Reserve Yards

Storage facilities containing reserve vessels and emergency supplies.

---

# Military Culture

Fort Robespierre produces officers with a distinctive reputation.

Traits commonly associated with graduates include:

- Discipline
- Aggressiveness
- Professionalism
- Political reliability

Critics occasionally add:

- Arrogance

The graduates generally do not disagree.

---

# Security

Security is among the strictest on Jardin.

Organizations present include:

## Naval Security Command

Primary military police force.

Responsible for:

- Internal investigations
- Base security
- Criminal enforcement

---

## Marine Provost Corps

Handles discipline among marine units.

Feared throughout the installation.

---

## SolSec Military Liaison Office

Maintains oversight regarding:

- Espionage
- Treason
- Strategic intelligence

---

## Social Solidarity Military Affairs Bureau

Ensures continued political reliability among personnel.

Its presence is often resented.

---

# Relationship with Other Institutions

## Palace of Continuity

Officially subordinate.

Unofficially influential.

Many officers believe politicians underestimate military realities.

---

## Social Solidarity

A necessary relationship.

Not always a comfortable one.

---

## Transstar

Maintains extensive logistics contracts with the military.

Many supplies pass through Transstar networks.

---

# Notable Landmarks

## Hall of Heroes

Massive memorial honoring fallen servicemen and women.

Every graduating class visits.

---

## Victory Square

Site of military parades and public ceremonies.

---

## The Iron Gate

The ceremonial entrance to the installation.

Named after the Iron Gate naval expansion program.

---

## Marshal Cartier Academy

Prestigious officer training institution.

Named after Marine Cartier.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Admiral Henri Duval

**Commander, Jardin Naval Command**

Senior naval officer assigned to the system.

A strong advocate for expanding naval power in the Solomani Front.

---

## General Claire Moreau

**Commander, Marine Forces Jardin**

Responsible for all marine training and deployment operations.

Widely respected by frontline personnel.

---

## Colonel Victor Almeida

**Commandant, Mech Combat Academy**

One of the Confederation's foremost experts on walker warfare.

A hero among mech pilots.

---

## Commander Lucien Marchand

**Director, Orbital Transfer Operations**

Controls military transportation between surface and orbit.

Few officers influence daily operations more.

---

# Public Reputation

Citizens generally view the Fort favorably.

Supporters see it as:

- A source of security
- A source of employment
- A symbol of national pride

Critics worry about its growing political influence.

Military officers tend to dismiss such concerns.

---

# Rumors

## The Deep Vault

A classified facility beneath the Annex contains military technology recovered from pre-Confederation ruins.

---

## Project Bastion

An experimental walker program is operating under extreme secrecy.

---

## The Lost Regiment

Records indicate an entire marine regiment disappeared during the Rim War.

Official histories never mention them.

---

## The Black Hangar

A secure aerospace facility receives unidentified vessels under heavy guard.

No official explanation exists.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing Prototype

An experimental combat walker disappears during field testing.

### Marine Investigation

A recruit dies under suspicious circumstances during training.

### Orbital Emergency

A military transport fails while transferring personnel to orbit.

### Classified Cargo

The Travellers are assigned to escort sensitive equipment through the Annex.

### Fleet Exercise

A training operation uncovers evidence of sabotage.

### The Black Hangar

A SolSec contact requests assistance investigating rumors surrounding a secret aerospace project.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

Fort Robespierre should feel like the martial soul of Jardin.

The Palace governs.

The Hall plans.

The Gardens study.

Social Solidarity enforces.

Transstar enriches.

The Fort prepares for war.

This is where the Confederation's ideals become military doctrine and where the next generation of officers, marines, and mech pilots learn how to defend the Solomani Cause.

For military characters, Fort Robespierre should feel like home.

For civilians, it should feel slightly intimidating.

For enemies of Jardin, it should feel dangerous.

GM-only truth goes here.
:::

title: Friday Party Congress Hearing
summary: Upcoming internal hearing where contacts may reveal who is shaping the official story after Mercier's death.
text:
# Friday Party Congress Hearing

## Overview

The Friday Party Congress hearing is expected two days after the [[Mercier Assassination]]. [[Alain Mercier]] had planned to reveal his findings there before he was killed.

[[Cass Pien (Kolb)]] contacted a trusted ally to listen from inside the hearing and report who speaks, what story is told, and which political players are involved.

## Why It Matters

- Mercier's intended presentation may still shape the agenda.
- The official story may reveal who benefits from the [[Mercier Frame-Up]].
- Names and arguments raised at the hearing could point toward the faction or individual who silenced Mercier.

## Open Questions

- Will Mercier's actual findings be suppressed, altered, or replaced?
- Who will speak for the official investigation?
- Will the [[Jardin Syndicate]] be blamed directly?
- Will the player characters be publicly named as an assassin cell?

title: Hall des Futurs Choisis
text:
# Hall des Futurs Choisis

## Hall of Chosen Futures

**Location:** Government District, Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin

**Type:** Ministry Complex, Civic Registry, Genetic Administration Center

**Population:** Approximately 120,000 employees and visitors daily

**Administrative Authority:** Ministry of Genetics

**Current Minister:** Alain Mercier

**Nickname:** *"The Hall"*

---

# Overview

The **Hall des Futurs Choisis** (Hall of Chosen Futures) is among the most influential institutions on Jardin and arguably the most important government building outside the Palace of Continuity itself.

Officially, the Hall exists to coordinate the scientific, educational, demographic, and civic planning efforts that have guided Jardin's development for centuries.

In practice, the Hall serves as the point where government policy intersects with everyday life.

Every citizen on Jardin will interact with the Hall repeatedly throughout their lifetime.

Birth registrations, educational assessments, citizenship certifications, genetic consultations, robot registrations, family planning permits, immigration reviews, military aptitude evaluations, and countless other services are administered through its vast bureaucracy.

For many citizens, the Hall represents the triumph of science and reason over chaos.

For many foreigners, it represents something far more unsettling.

---

# Public Purpose

The Hall's official mission is inscribed above the main entrance:

> "The Future Is Not Found. It Is Built."

The institution exists to help citizens make informed decisions that contribute to both personal success and societal prosperity.

Government publications describe the Hall as:

* A center of scientific guidance
* A guardian of public welfare
* A steward of future generations
* A repository of civic records
* A facilitator of social opportunity

Critics throughout the Confederation often describe it as:

* A surveillance apparatus
* A demographic control center
* A social engineering institution

Most citizens reject such characterizations.

---

# History

## Foundation Era

The original Hall began as a simple census and population records office during Jardin's first century.

As population expanded, government planners discovered that accurate demographic forecasting provided significant economic advantages.

Successive administrations gradually expanded the office's authority.

What began as a registry evolved into a planning bureau.

The planning bureau evolved into a ministry.

The ministry eventually became the Hall.

---

## The Genetic Reforms

The modern Hall emerged during the Genetic Reforms of the sixth century.

Advances in biological sciences allowed officials to:

* Predict hereditary disorders
* Track population trends
* Improve public health outcomes
* Guide educational development

These programs proved remarkably successful.

Life expectancy increased.

Crime declined.

Educational outcomes improved.

The public generally supported further expansion.

---

## The Integration Period

During the following centuries dozens of independent agencies were merged into the Hall.

Functions absorbed included:

* Birth registration
* Citizenship management
* Immigration processing
* Marriage licensing
* Educational placement
* Robot registration
* Population statistics
* Genetic counseling

By the modern era, nearly every major life event passed through the Hall.

---

# Architecture

The Hall covers nearly four square kilometers and consists of seven interconnected administrative towers surrounding a central civic plaza.

Unlike the grandeur of the Palace of Continuity, the Hall was designed to appear approachable.

Its architecture emphasizes:

* Transparency
* Efficiency
* Accessibility
* Scientific progress

Visitors enter through enormous public atriums filled with:

* Trees
* Gardens
* Public art
* Information kiosks
* Educational exhibits

The atmosphere resembles a university campus more than a government ministry.

Only after proceeding deeper into the complex does its true scale become apparent.

---

# Major Divisions

## Citizen Registry Directorate

The oldest department within the Hall.

Responsible for maintaining records on every legal resident of Jardin.

Files contain:

* Birth records
* Family history
* Educational records
* Employment history
* Military service
* Citizenship classification

Many records extend back generations.

Officials proudly describe the Registry as the most complete demographic database in the Confederation.

---

## Bureau of Genetic Assessment

Perhaps the Hall's most famous department.

Provides:

* Genetic screening
* Health forecasting
* Hereditary risk analysis
* Family planning consultation

Participation is technically voluntary.

Nearly everyone participates.

Citizens often view refusing assessment as irresponsible.

---

## Directorate of Educational Futures

Tracks educational development from childhood through adulthood.

Responsibilities include:

* Aptitude testing
* School placement
* Scholarship recommendations
* Professional forecasting

Students are not assigned careers.

However, guidance recommendations are considered highly reliable.

Many citizens follow them.

---

## Bureau of Family Development

Handles:

* Marriage registrations
* Family planning consultations
* Adoption reviews
* Child development programs

This bureau generates much of the Hall's reputation outside Jardin.

Imperial media frequently exaggerates its role.

In reality, most services remain advisory rather than mandatory.

---

## Immigration and Citizenship Authority

Responsible for:

* Immigration processing
* Naturalization
* Residency permits
* Foreign worker authorization

Every long-term visitor eventually encounters this department.

---

## Robot Registration Bureau

One of the Hall's newer divisions.

Every autonomous robot on Jardin must be registered.

Records include:

* Ownership
* Operational classification
* Maintenance history
* Intelligence certification
* Security reviews

Visitors arriving with robots are required to register them within seven days.

Military-grade machines receive additional scrutiny.

Unregistered robots can be confiscated.

---

# The Citizen Lifecycle

A typical citizen may interact with the Hall dozens of times throughout life.

## Birth

Parents register newborns.

Genetic records are established.

Healthcare recommendations are provided.

---

## Childhood

Educational assessments begin.

Development milestones are recorded.

Specialized opportunities are identified.

---

## Adolescence

Aptitude evaluations occur.

Career counseling becomes available.

Military and civic service pathways are discussed.

---

## Adulthood

Marriage registration.

Family planning consultation.

Professional certifications.

Property records.

Citizenship updates.

---

## Retirement

Medical support planning.

Historical record preservation.

Legacy documentation.

---

# Daily Operations

Nearly 500,000 people pass through the Hall every week.

Despite this volume, waiting times remain remarkably short.

Visitors frequently report:

* Professional staff
* Efficient processing
* Friendly service
* Exceptional organization

Foreigners often find the experience unnerving.

Questions seem prepared before they are asked.

Records appear instantly.

Appointments begin precisely on schedule.

Officials already know what forms are required.

---

# Minister Alain Mercier

Current Minister of Genetics and director of the Hall.

Mercier is widely considered one of the most influential individuals on Jardin.

Educated at the National Academy of Biological Sciences, he spent decades overseeing demographic planning initiatives before ascending to ministerial leadership.

Supporters view him as:

* A visionary scientist
* A reformer
* A defender of evidence-based policy

Critics accuse him of:

* Excessive technocratic thinking
* Overreliance on predictive systems
* Expanding state influence into private life

His upcoming presentation at the next Party Congress has generated enormous public interest.

---

# Public Opinion

Surveys consistently show strong public approval.

Citizens commonly cite:

* Efficient services
* Improved healthcare
* Better educational outcomes
* Reduced bureaucracy

A common Jardin saying states:

> "The Hall knows where the forms are."

Meaning that even complicated problems usually have a solution.

---

# Common Sayings

### "Trust the numbers."

Often used when discussing Hall recommendations.

### "The Hall remembers."

Refers to the vast citizen archives.

### "Everyone visits eventually."

A common joke about the institution's reach.

### "Ask the Hall."

Used when seeking official guidance.

---

# Security

Security is extensive but intentionally discreet.

Visible measures include:

* Civil security officers
* Automated scanners
* Visitor registration systems

Less visible measures include:

* Internal intelligence personnel
* Data monitoring teams
* Behavioral analysis systems
* Counterespionage units

The Hall maintains one of the most valuable information repositories in the Confederation.

---

# Foreign Visitors

Visitors remaining longer than seven days must register.

The process is usually straightforward.

Typical requirements include:

* Identity verification
* Ship registration
* Residence declaration
* Robot registration
* Temporary citizen file creation

Most visitors complete the process within an hour.

Many leave feeling uncomfortable without being entirely certain why.

---

# Rumors

Numerous rumors surround the Hall.

Common stories include:

* The government maintains predictive profiles on every citizen.
* Secret archives contain centuries of genetic data.
* Certain future leaders are identified during childhood.
* The Hall can predict career success with startling accuracy.
* Hidden departments monitor social stability across the planet.

Officials dismiss such stories as exaggerations.

---

# Classified Information

The following information is not publicly known.

## Project Horizon

A classified demographic forecasting initiative operating beneath the Hall.

Project Horizon attempts to model Jardin's future decades in advance.

Economic, educational, political, and biological data are continuously analyzed.

Officially, the project does not exist.

---

## Restricted Archives

Deep beneath the Hall lies a secure repository containing genetic records dating back centuries.

Access is limited to a handful of senior officials.

Even many Hall administrators are unaware of its existence.

---

## The Mercier Findings

Recent research conducted under Minister Alain Mercier has produced highly controversial results.

The exact nature of these findings remains unknown.

However, rumors have spread throughout government circles that the data may challenge long-standing assumptions underlying Jardin's social planning philosophy.

Several senior officials reportedly view the upcoming Party Congress with considerable anxiety.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing File

A citizen's entire history vanishes from the Registry.

Officially this should be impossible.

### Ghost Citizen

Records indicate a person who has never existed.

Yet dozens of government databases recognize them.

### Stolen Genome

A classified genetic profile is stolen from the Restricted Archives.

Several intelligence agencies become interested.

### Foreign Defector

A Hall researcher seeks passage off-world.

They claim to possess evidence capable of destabilizing the government.

### Robot Uprising

Thousands of robot registrations suddenly contain identical authorization codes.

No one can explain why.

### The Wrong Future

A prominent citizen receives a predictive assessment describing events that have not yet happened.

Months later, those events begin occurring.

### Party Congress

The Travellers become entangled in the political fallout surrounding Alain Mercier's long-awaited presentation.

## Session 1 - Official Business

The player characters were summoned here for administrative reasons tied to genetic status, military records, finances, or autonomous AI review. The process resembled a large bureaucratic office: numbers were assigned, people were called in strange orders, and military IDs were checked because many arrived armed.

The grouping was suspicious. Too many armed veterans and connected figures were brought together in one area, and several already knew each other from prior service.

Shortly after a news broadcast about [[Alain Mercier]] and genetic-file corruption, Mercier crossed the office area and was killed in the [[Mercier Assassination]].

## Session 1 Security Notes

- The Hall had cameras throughout the office area.
- Government workers used bioscans to open secure side doors.
- [[Home Guard]] and [[Social Solidarity]] personnel responded quickly.
- A walker/mech later breached the window during the group's escape.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

The Hall des Futurs Choisis should not be portrayed as overtly sinister.

The institution is most effective when it appears competent, rational, and largely beneficial.

The discomfort comes not from oppression but from capability.

Everything works.

The records are accurate.

The recommendations are useful.

The staff are courteous.

The government genuinely improves many lives.

The question facing visitors is whether any institution should possess this much information, this much influence, and this much confidence in its ability to shape the future.

That question lies at the heart of modern Jardin.

GM-only truth goes here.
:::
