title: Mercier Frame-Up
summary: The official story falsely blamed the Mercier assassination on the player characters and genetic outcasts.
text:
# Mercier Frame-Up

## Overview

After [[Alain Mercier]] was killed, the official narrative rapidly reframed the event as a terrorist action by genetic "half-breeds" whose genetic identities were supposedly insecure. The news story included fabricated or edited footage, including a false image of [[Zoy (Damon)]] throwing a grenade at Mercier.

The frame-up also accused the group of killing Mercier's bodyguards. Some of that claim is misleading rather than wholly invented: the group did kill Home Guard personnel during the escape, but after the situation had already turned violent and after Emma received a warning that the authorities intended to kill them.

## Why the Group Was Vulnerable

- The characters were gathered in one room despite several having prior military connections.
- They were allowed in armed because military veterans and retired service personnel may carry weapons with proper IDs.
- Several already knew one another, making it easy to portray them as an existing cell.
- Their mixed backgrounds, non-human members, and criminal or intelligence ties made them easy propaganda targets.

## Evidence and Counter-Evidence

- [[Marcel Delphine]] says the [[Jardin Syndicate]] has unedited footage.
- [[Emma Colbert (Laura)]] has a recording of the "kill them all" warning/order from her setup.
- Mercier's locked pad may contain relevant evidence.
- The public footage is known to include fake material.

## Open Questions

- Who built the false news package so quickly?
- Who ordered Social Solidarity or Home Guard to kill the witnesses?
- What will [[Elena Verona]] say as the surviving commissar?
- Will the Friday hearing reveal who benefits from the cover story?

## Overview

Mercier's locked pad was recovered from [[Alain Mercier]] after his assassination. It is roughly Kindle-sized and password protected.

- An attempted hack failed.
- Four attempts remain before the pad wipes itself.

title: Miasma
summary: A low-key destination one jump from Jardin, considered as a possible escape target.
text:
# Miasma

## Overview

Miasma is described in the transcript as a relatively low-key jump destination one jump from [[Jardin]]. [[Judith Dumont (Carla)]] considered it as a possible escape destination and hoped [[Keith Morris]] could help with plans to get there.

## Session 1 Notes

- Directly jumping from Jardin is difficult because of passport and identity controls.
- Keith Morris advised that moving to another planet within the Jardin system first may be easier than leaving directly from Jardin.
- Miasma remains a possible later destination once identity or transit problems are solved.

title: Port Sainte-Jeanne Downport
text:
# Port Sainte-Jeanne Downport

*"Gateway to the Frontier."*

**Location:** Western Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

**Type:** Class A Starport Downport

**Administrative Authority:** Port Authority of Jardin

**Operational Control:** Transstar Port Services

**Security:** Port Security Directorate, Customs Bureau, Social Solidarity, SolSec Liaison Office

**Population:** 3.5 Million Permanent Residents

**Daily Population:** 10-20 Million

**Nickname:** The Port

---

# Overview

The **Port Sainte-Jeanne Downport** is the primary surface gateway to Jardin and one of the busiest starports in the Solomani Front.

Every day thousands of passengers and millions of tons of cargo pass through its terminals.

The Port serves as the physical connection between:

- Jardin
- The Solomani Confederation
- The Imperial Border
- The Hive Federation
- The Frontier Worlds

Most visitors see the Palace of Continuity as the symbol of Jardin.

Most residents know the truth.

The Port keeps the world alive.

---

# First Impressions

The first thing visitors notice is the scale.

The second is the noise.

The third is the smell.

Fuel.

Industrial lubricants.

Imported foods.

 Ocean air.

Humanity.

The Port never truly sleeps.

Ships arrive at all hours.

Cargo moves continuously.

Announcements echo from towering terminals.

Some visitors love it immediately.

Others never feel comfortable here.

---

# History

## The Original Landing Field

The first colonial settlement on Jardin was established near the present-day Port.

Its founders were:

- Entrepreneurs
- Soldiers
- Adventurers
- Political dissidents

Many arrived specifically to escape Imperial authority.

Their descendants still celebrate this history.

---

## The Expansion Era

As Jardin built its early empire, the Port expanded dramatically.

Military shipyards.

Colonial fleets.

 Trade convoys.

Occupation forces.

All departed from Port Sainte-Jeanne.

Some historians argue the Port was the true heart of the old Jardinese Empire.

---

## Modern Era

Today the Port remains one of the largest transportation hubs in the subsector.

Its facilities support:

- Commercial traffic
- Naval logistics
- Colonial development
- Diplomatic missions
- Frontier exploration

---

# Layout

The Port covers hundreds of square kilometers.

Major sections include:

## Arrival Terminal Complex

Passenger processing center.

Most visitors begin their journey here.

Contains:

- Customs
- Immigration
- Transportation links
- Hotels
- Currency exchanges

The terminal handles millions of travelers every month.

---

## Cargo Basin Alpha

The largest freight handling district.

Dominated by:

- Warehouses
- Cargo cranes
- Freight towers
- Container yards

Thousands of workers operate here around the clock.

---

## Colonial Transit Zone

Specialized facilities serving:

- Frontier Colonies
- Development Projects
- Settlement Programs

Many citizens beginning new lives pass through this district.

It has become known as:

> The District of Second Chances

---

## Orbital Elevator Complex

A massive transportation system linking the downport to Port Sainte-Jeanne Highport.

The elevators operate continuously.

Most cargo reaching orbit passes through these towers.

---

## Naval Interface Sector

Joint facilities supporting:

- Confederation Navy
- Home Forces
- Military Logistics

Public access is heavily restricted.

---

# The Port Authority

The Port is administered by the **Port Authority of Jardin**.

Responsibilities include:

- Traffic Control
- Safety Enforcement
- Infrastructure Management
- Customs Coordination

The Authority works closely with:

- Transstar
- Social Solidarity
- SolSec
- Confederation Navy

---

# Security

The Port contains more law enforcement personnel than any other location on Jardin.

Security organizations include:

## Port Security Directorate

Primary law enforcement agency.

Handles:

- Smuggling
- Violent crime
- Port security
- Cargo theft

---

## Customs Bureau

Responsible for:

- Import controls
- Export inspections
- Contraband interdiction

Many traders fear Customs more than Port Security.

---

## Social Solidarity

Monitors:

- Returning citizens
- Immigration concerns
- Ideological risks

Visitors often encounter them without realizing it.

---

## SolSec Liaison Office

Handles:

- Foreign intelligence threats
- Espionage
- Counter-terrorism

Officially a small office.

Nobody believes this.

---

# Culture

The Port is one of the least typical places on Jardin.

People from across Charted Space pass through daily.

Visitors include:

- Merchants
- Colonists
- Diplomats
- Mercenaries
- Explorers
- Refugees

As a result the Port possesses a reputation for being unusually open-minded.

At least by Jardinese standards.

---

# Neighborhoods

## The Sailor's Quarter

The oldest district in the Port.

Filled with:

- Taverns
- Boarding Houses
- Restaurants
- Entertainment Venues

A favorite destination for ship crews.

---

## Republic Market

A sprawling commercial bazaar.

Goods from hundreds of worlds can be found here.

Many items are legal.

Most are.

---

## Founder's Row

Historic district preserving buildings from the original colony.

Popular with tourists.

---

## The Freightlands

Industrial district serving cargo workers.

A rough area after dark.

---

# Organized Crime

The Port has always attracted criminal organizations.

Common activities include:

- Smuggling
- Cargo theft
- Identity fraud
- Illegal migration
- Contraband trafficking

The Jardin Syndicate maintains significant influence throughout the Port.

---

# The Syndicate Presence

The Port serves as one of the Syndicate's most important operational zones.

Common activities include:

- Forged travel documents
- Unofficial shipping services
- Labor rackets
- Black-market logistics

Port Security fights a constant battle against them.

Neither side expects victory.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Director Luc Beaumont

**Port Authority Director**

Responsible for overseeing the largest transportation facility on Jardin.

Pragmatic and politically skilled.

---

## Commander Sophie Durand

**Port Security Directorate**

Veteran investigator known for her relentless pursuit of organized crime.

---

## Étienne Marchand

**Customs Commissioner**

A nightmare for smugglers.

A hero to accountants.

---

## Captain Victor Mendes

**Transstar Freight Captain**

A respected merchant captain known throughout the Port community.

---

# Public Reputation

Citizens often describe the Port as:

> The Real Jardin

Unlike the carefully managed government districts, the Port feels alive.

Messy.

Loud.

Unpredictable.

Successful.

---

# Rumors

## The Hidden Berth

An unregistered landing field allegedly exists beneath the Port.

---

## The Ghost Manifest

A cargo record from the Solomani Rim War appears in the system every year despite the ship having been destroyed centuries ago.

---

## The Shepherd's Dock

A private landing zone supposedly controlled by Marcel Delphine.

No one can prove it exists.

---

## The Missing Containers

Entire cargo shipments occasionally vanish without explanation.

Port officials deny this happens.

Nobody believes them.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing Passenger

A prominent scientist disappears after arriving at the terminal.

### Customs Seizure

A cargo container contains something far more dangerous than declared.

### Syndicate Courier

The Travellers are mistaken for criminal couriers.

### Naval Investigation

Military cargo disappears from the Naval Interface Sector.

### The Ghost Ship

A vessel presumed lost decades ago suddenly requests docking clearance.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

A message directs the Travellers to an unmarked warehouse in the Freightlands.

Someone is waiting.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

Port Sainte-Jeanne should feel very different from the rest of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

The Palace is power.

The Hall is bureaucracy.

The Gardens are science.

Rousseau is society.

The Exchange is money.

The Port is movement.

Everything arrives here.

Everything leaves here.

Ideas.

Goods.

People.

Dreams.

Criminals.

Refugees.

Spies.

For many visitors, Port Sainte-Jeanne is their first experience of Jardin.

For many citizens, it is their last view of home.

The Port is where the world meets the stars.

GM-only truth goes here.
:::

A private landing zone supposedly controlled by Marcel Delphine.

No one can prove it exists.

---

## The Missing Containers

Entire cargo shipments occasionally vanish without explanation.

Port officials deny this happens.

Nobody believes them.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing Passenger

A prominent scientist disappears after arriving at the terminal.

### Customs Seizure

A cargo container contains something far more dangerous than declared.

### Syndicate Courier

The Travellers are mistaken for criminal couriers.

### Naval Investigation

Military cargo disappears from the Naval Interface Sector.

### The Ghost Ship

A vessel presumed lost decades ago suddenly requests docking clearance.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

A message directs the Travellers to an unmarked warehouse in the Freightlands.

Someone is waiting.

---

title: Quartier Danton
text:
# Quartier Danton
# Danton Quarter

*"Ideas Shape History."*

**Location:** Central Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

**Type:** Political and Academic District

**Administrative Authority:** Municipal Government of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

**Status:** Active

**Population:** 2.4 Million

**Primary Functions:**
- Party Administration
- Universities
- Think Tanks
- Media Organizations
- Political Clubs
- Civic Associations

**Nickname:** The Quarter

---

# Overview

The **Quartier Danton** serves as the intellectual and political center of Jardin.

While the Palace of Continuity governs the planet and the Hall of Chosen Futures manages its citizens, Danton is where competing visions of the future are debated.

The district contains:

- Universities
- Party schools
- Newspapers
- Research institutes
- Policy centers
- Public forums

Most major political movements on Jardin begin here.

Many also die here.

---

# First Impressions

Danton feels educated.

Bookstores line the streets.

Students crowd cafés.

 Professors argue in public squares.

 Political posters cover walls.

 News screens display debates twenty-four hours a day.

Visitors quickly discover that people in Danton have opinions.

Strong opinions.

Usually several at once.

---

# History

## The Founding

The district emerged around Jardin's earliest universities.

After the Solomani Party assumed power, numerous political schools and research institutes were established nearby.

Over time the area became the unofficial center of political thought.

---

## The Congress District

Following the violent Party Congress of 844, many political organizations centralized their operations in Danton.

The district gradually evolved into a permanent political marketplace.

Not for votes.

For influence.

---

# Architecture

Unlike the monumental government districts, Danton is designed on a human scale.

Features include:

- Broad boulevards
- Public squares
- Lecture halls
- Libraries
- Cafés
- Academic courtyards

The atmosphere resembles a university city more than a capital district.

---

# Major Districts

## Republic Square

The symbolic center of the Quarter.

Location of:

- Public speeches
- Demonstrations
- Party events
- Academic debates

Nearly every political movement on Jardin has held rallies here.

---

## Academy Row

Home to:

- Universities
- Research Institutes
- Party Academies

Thousands of students study here.

---

## The Press District

Contains the headquarters of:

- Jardin Today
- State Broadcasting Services
- Academic Journals
- Independent Publications

Journalists wield considerable influence within Danton.

---

## Forum Boulevard

A long avenue lined with:

- Think Tanks
- Policy Institutes
- Civic Organizations

Every building appears to be studying something.

---

## The Debate Gardens

Public park designed specifically for lectures, discussions, and political gatherings.

An unusual feature even by Solomani standards.

---

# Political Culture

Politics in Danton is intense.

Most debates occur between competing interpretations of the Solomani Cause.

Common factions include:

### Continuity Bloc

Supports stability and existing institutions.

Associated with Leo Duprey.

---

### Future Movement

Advocates scientific planning and social innovation.

Associated with Alain Mercier.

---

### Civic Humanists

Support greater local autonomy and civic participation.

Viewed with suspicion by some hardliners.

---

### Frontier Development League

Promotes expansion, colonization, and economic growth.

Strong support from Transstar interests.

---

# Universities

## University of Jardin

The most prestigious educational institution on the planet.

Produces:

- Scientists
- Administrators
- Diplomats

Many future leaders begin here.

---

## Party Academy of Public Administration

The premier training institution for future Party officials.

Graduates dominate government service.

---

## Institute for Historical Continuity

Responsible for preserving and interpreting the history of Jardin.

Frequently accused of political bias.

---

# Security

The district remains heavily monitored.

However, security is often subtle.

Organizations present include:

- Social Solidarity
- Campus Security
- SolSec Liaison Offices

Most activity focuses on observation rather than intervention.

---

# Relationship with Social Solidarity

Complicated.

Many academics depend upon government funding.

Many academics also criticize government policy.

Both sides tolerate each other.

Most of the time.

---

# Relationship with the Ministry of Genetics

Very close.

Researchers from the Closed Gardens frequently lecture and teach in Danton.

Many Ministry officials maintain offices here.

---

# Notable Locations

## The Hall of Public Discourse

A massive amphitheater used for debates and conferences.

---

## Café République

Most famous political café on Jardin.

Hundreds of careers have started here.

Many have ended here as well.

---

## Library of the Solomani Future

One of the largest public libraries in the Confederation.

---

## Monument to the First Congress

Commemorates the founding generation of the Solomani Movement.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Professor Émile Laurent

Political theorist and public intellectual.

Frequently appears on state broadcasts.

---

## Juliette Mercier

Senior editor at Jardin Today.

Known for asking difficult questions.

---

## Director Alain Mercier

Maintains an office in the district despite serving primarily from the Closed Gardens.

His lectures routinely fill entire auditoriums.

---

## Pierre Gauthier

Student activist and rising political figure.

Closely watched by Social Solidarity.

---

# Public Reputation

Most citizens describe Danton as:

> The place where people talk.

Supporters consider it the conscience of Jardin.

Critics consider it a district full of people who have never held a real job.

---

# Rumors

## The Secret Congress

Influential figures occasionally meet in private to shape future Party policy.

---

## The Missing Manuscript

A controversial political work disappeared before publication.

---

## The Speaker's List

Certain lecturers are quietly prevented from speaking in public.

Officially no such list exists.

---

## The Forgotten Archive

Records from the violent Congress of 844 remain sealed somewhere beneath the district.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Stolen Speech

A major Party address is leaked before delivery.

### Academic Defector

A visiting scholar vanishes after announcing controversial findings.

### Congress Intrigue

Multiple factions attempt to influence an upcoming Party Congress.

### The Lost Archive

Evidence emerges regarding events surrounding Marine Cartier's rise to power.

### Student Movement

A campus protest begins attracting attention from Social Solidarity.

### The Future Report

An unpublished Ministry document threatens to destabilize several powerful factions.

---
:::gm

# Referee Notes

Danton should feel like the ideological battlefield of Jardin.

Not a place of riots and revolution.

A place of arguments.

Every café hosts debates.

Every university produces theories.

Every faction believes it is saving civilization.

If Fort Robespierre trains soldiers and the Closed Gardens train scientists, Danton trains politicians.

And on Jardin, that can be just as dangerous.

:::gm
GM-only truth goes here.
:::

title: Quartier Sainte-Liberté
text:
# Quartier Sainte-Liberté
# Saint Liberty Quarter

*"Everyone Belongs Somewhere."*

**Location:** Lower Eastern Rousseau Arcology and Adjacent Surface Districts

**Type:** Mixed Residential and Commercial District

**Administrative Authority:** Municipal Administration of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne

**Actual Influence:** Jardin Syndicate, Local Associations, Independent Commercial Networks

**Population:** 4.7 Million

**Status:** Active

**Primary Functions:**
- Entertainment
- Nightlife
- Independent Commerce
- Immigrant Communities
- Grey-Market Trade
- Hospitality

**Nickname:** Sainte-Lib

---

# Overview

The **Quartier Sainte-Liberté** is the most vibrant, chaotic, and controversial district on Jardin.

To government officials, it is an economic necessity.

To tourists, it is fascinating.

To Social Solidarity, it is a headache.

To the Syndicate, it is home.

Unlike the carefully planned districts that dominate the rest of Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Sainte-Liberté grew organically over centuries.

The result is a dense urban labyrinth of:

- Neon signs
- Elevated transit lines
- Markets
- Nightclubs
- Data cafés
- Capsule hotels
- Apartment towers
- Hidden courtyards

The district never truly sleeps.

At any hour one can find food, music, business, crime, and opportunity.

Many visitors compare it to stepping into another world.

---

# First Impressions

Sainte-Liberté assaults the senses.

Giant holographic advertisements float between towers.

Light reflects from perpetual rain and condensation.

Transit trains roar overhead.

Thousands of conversations fill the air.

The scent of:

- Street food
- Industrial machinery
- Ocean air
- Imported spices

mixes together into something uniquely Sainte-Liberté.

Officially Jardin is orderly.

Sainte-Liberté is what happens when millions of people improvise.

---

# History

## The Port Expansion

The district originated as worker housing supporting the expansion of Port Sainte-Jeanne.

Dock workers.

Freighter crews.

Construction laborers.

Merchants.

Immigrants.

Over time these populations created their own communities.

---

## The Arcology Connection

When Rousseau expanded, elevated transit corridors linked the district directly to the arcology.

Development exploded.

Commercial towers grew upward.

Markets expanded.

Entertainment venues multiplied.

The district became the unofficial social capital of the city.

---

## Modern Era

Today Sainte-Liberté occupies a unique position.

It is not poor.

It is not wealthy.

It is not controlled.

At least not completely.

---

# Architecture

The district is characterized by:

- Dense construction
- Vertical development
- Narrow streets
- Endless signage
- Layered infrastructure

Buildings have been expanded and modified repeatedly over centuries.

Many structures contain:

- Retail space
- Apartments
- Workshops
- Restaurants
- Data centers

within the same tower.

The skyline is dominated by holographic advertising and elevated transportation systems.

---

# Atmosphere

The district feels unlike anywhere else on Jardin.

Most of the planet projects:

- Confidence
- Order
- Control

Sainte-Liberté projects:

- Energy
- Ambition
- Survival

People come here to build lives.

Or hide from them.

---

# Major Districts

## Neon Row

The entertainment heart of Sainte-Liberté.

Contains:

- Clubs
- Bars
- Music venues
- Casinos
- Performance halls

Bright enough to be visible from orbit.

---

## Market des Étoiles
### Star Market

The largest open commercial market on Jardin.

Goods from across the Confederation can be found here.

If you cannot find it in the Star Market, it probably does not exist.

---

## The Rainwalks

A maze of elevated pedestrian pathways.

Lined with:

- Food stalls
- Small shops
- Street performers

Popular with tourists.

Also popular with pickpockets.

---

## Lantern Quarter

One of the oldest neighborhoods in the city.

Known for:

- Traditional architecture
- Family-owned businesses
- Community organizations

Remarkably resistant to redevelopment.

---

## Foundry Ward

Industrial-commercial district.

Contains:

- Fabrication shops
- Robotics repair
- Vehicle modification
- Technical services

Many independent technicians operate here.

---

# Commerce

The district hosts millions of independent businesses.

Unlike the Exchange, where corporate wealth dominates, Sainte-Liberté belongs to:

- Small business owners
- Entrepreneurs
- Traders
- Artisans

The economy is dynamic, unpredictable, and difficult to regulate.

---

# The Data Markets

One of the district's most unusual features.

Information is bought and sold constantly.

Services include:

- Research
- Market analysis
- Reputation tracking
- Historical archives

Most operations are legal.

Most.

---

# Nightlife

The district's nightlife is legendary.

Visitors can find:

- Jazz clubs
- Holographic theaters
- Music halls
- Virtual experience lounges

Many establishments remain open twenty-four hours a day.

---

# The Syndicate Presence

The Jardin Syndicate maintains extensive influence throughout Sainte-Liberté.

Their involvement ranges from:

- Protection services
- Dispute mediation
- Labor organization
- Smuggling

Many residents view the Syndicate as more reliable than official authorities.

Publicly, they would never admit this.

---

# Social Solidarity Presence

The Ministry maintains numerous offices throughout the district.

Relations with residents are often complicated.

Social Solidarity can enforce laws.

It cannot easily enforce conformity.

This frustrates some officials.

---

# Crime

Common criminal activity includes:

- Smuggling
- Identity fraud
- Contraband sales
- Corporate espionage
- Illegal gambling

Despite its reputation, violent crime remains relatively low.

The Syndicate has strong incentives to keep business stable.

---

# Notable Locations

## The Azure Dragon

Most famous restaurant in the district.

A favored meeting place for merchants and smugglers alike.

---

## Liberty Station

Largest transit hub in Sainte-Liberté.

More than two million passengers pass through daily.

---

## The Holo Gardens

An entertainment complex combining public parks and holographic art installations.

---

## The Crescent Market

The largest night market on Jardin.

Open every evening.

Never entirely closes.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Marcel Delphine

**The Shepherd**

Although he officially does not live here, many believe Sainte-Liberté is the true center of his influence.

---

## Colette Moreau

**President, Liberty Merchants Association**

One of the most influential civilian leaders in the district.

---

## Lucien Roche

**Information Broker**

Can acquire almost any information.

For the right price.

---

## Inspector Adrien Martin

**Social Solidarity Liaison**

Responsible for maintaining relations between local organizations and the government.

His job is extremely difficult.

---

# Public Reputation

Citizens from elsewhere on Jardin often describe Sainte-Liberté as:

> Too loud.
>
> Too crowded.
>
> Too free.

Residents generally consider this a compliment.

---

# Rumors

## The Hidden Exchange

A secret market exists beneath the district where anything can be bought.

Anything.

---

## The Shepherd's Court

A hidden meeting place where disputes are resolved under Syndicate protection.

---

## Ghost Towers

Several abandoned towers supposedly contain forgotten infrastructure dating back centuries.

---

## The Black Ledger

Evidence exists proving corruption among major officials.

Numerous organizations are searching for it.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing Courier

A data courier vanishes before delivering sensitive information.

### Syndicate Arbitration

The Travellers are asked to mediate a dispute between competing criminal organizations.

### The Hidden Market

Rumors of a secret underground exchange prove true.

### Corporate Heist

A rival corporation hires the Travellers to steal valuable trade data.

### Ghost Tower

An abandoned structure suddenly begins transmitting signals.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

A message arrives requesting a meeting in Sainte-Liberté.

No address is provided.

Only a time.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

Sainte-Liberté should feel like the one place on Jardin where control becomes difficult.

The Palace controls politics.

The Hall controls futures.

The Gardens control knowledge.

Social Solidarity controls compliance.

But Sainte-Liberté controls culture.

This is where trends begin.

Where music spreads.

Where merchants gamble.

Where immigrants build new lives.

Where the Syndicate thrives.

If the rest of Jardin feels like a carefully designed machine, Sainte-Liberté should feel like humanity leaking out through the cracks.


GM-only truth goes here.
:::

> Too free.

Residents generally consider this a compliment.

---

# Rumors

## The Hidden Exchange

A secret market exists beneath the district where anything can be bought.

Anything.

---

## The Shepherd's Court

A hidden meeting place where disputes are resolved under Syndicate protection.

---

## Ghost Towers

Several abandoned towers supposedly contain forgotten infrastructure dating back centuries.

---

## The Black Ledger

Evidence exists proving corruption among major officials.

Numerous organizations are searching for it.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Missing Courier

A data courier vanishes before delivering sensitive information.

### Syndicate Arbitration

The Travellers are asked to mediate a dispute between competing criminal organizations.

### The Hidden Market

Rumors of a secret underground exchange prove true.

### Corporate Heist

A rival corporation hires the Travellers to steal valuable trade data.

### Ghost Tower

An abandoned structure suddenly begins transmitting signals.

### The Shepherd's Invitation

A message arrives requesting a meeting in Sainte-Liberté.

No address is provided.

Only a time.

---

title: Sarah Gay's Son
summary: Emma Colbert's smuggler contact; a mid-tier smuggler loyal to Emma within limits.
text:
# Sarah Gay's Son

## Overview

Sarah Gay's son is an unnamed mid-tier smuggler contact of [[Emma Colbert (Laura)]]. The transcript describes him as oddly loyal to Emma, provided she does not ask him to betray family or front crew.

He gave Emma background on [[Marcel Delphine]] and the [[Jardin Syndicate]], saying the Syndicate is unlikely to betray the group as long as they deal honestly while down and out.

## Appears In

- [[Session 1 - Official Business]]

## Information Provided

- The Jardin Syndicate often helps people who are less genetically pure by creating or altering genetic identities.
- The Syndicate is generally useful to the desperate or excluded.
- The [[Transstar Sector Exchange]] is an independent legal entity and potential refuge if the group's identity veils begin to fail.

## Human Review

The contact's personal name is not present in the transcript.

title: Session 1 - Official Business
summary: Opening session: the group is framed for Alain Mercier's assassination and rescued by Marcel Delphine.
text:
# Session 1 - Official Business

**Transcript Source:** `Stars g1.md`

## Synopsis

The group is summoned to the [[Hall des Futurs Choisis]] for official review and finds itself assembled in an unusually armed, suspiciously connected cluster. A broadcast announces that Minister [[Alain Mercier]] will reveal corruption in genetic records at the Party Congress. Moments later, Mercier is killed in front of them by shots that appear to implicate their own weapons.

The first response from [[Social Solidarity]] and the [[Home Guard]] becomes a firefight after [[Emma Colbert (Laura)]] receives a warning that the authorities intend to kill them. The group escapes using an intact walker/mech after [[0-31 Saichi Maru (Annette)]] kills its pilot, then is taken by a driverless grav speeder to [[Marcel Delphine]].

Marcel explains that the [[Jardin Syndicate]] wants the group alive because the official story may be used to blame both the characters and the Syndicate while burying Mercier's real findings. He provides [[Identity Veils]] and a one-use communicator, and offers unedited assassination footage in exchange for future cooperation.

## Important Pages

- [[Mercier Assassination]]
- [[Mercier Frame-Up]]
- [[False News Broadcast]]
- [[Identity Veils]]
- [[Mercier's Locked Pad]]
- [[Friday Party Congress Hearing]]
- [[Sunken Ship-Boat]]

## Characters Present

- [[0-31 Saichi Maru (Annette)]]
- [[Cass Pien (Kolb)]]
- [[Ciernan Seanislav (Peter)]]
- [[Dan Araujo (Josh)]]
- [[Emma Colbert (Laura)]]
- [[Judith Dumont (Carla)]]
- [[Varian Svazz (Allen)]]
- [[Victor Mendes (Steinbach)]]
- [[Zoy (Damon)]]

## Open Questions

- Who controlled or spoofed the weapons?
- What did Mercier know?
- Who is "above" the Syndicate in Marcel's warning?
- Can the group get unedited footage, unlock the pad, or reach the Friday hearing's inside information?
- Will the group clear its names, flee Jardin, or work with Marcel?

title: Social Solidarity
text:
# Ministère de la Solidarité Sociale

# Ministry of Social Solidarity

*"Harmony Through Responsibility"*

**Designation:** Ministry of Social Solidarity | **Type:** Government Ministry | **Founded:** 705 | **Status:** Active | **Authority:** Supreme Presidency of Jardin

Social Solidarity Emblem
![Ministry of Social Solidarity](/wiki/media/Ministry-of-Social-Solidarity.png)

| Field               | Value                         |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| Designation         | Ministry of Social Solidarity |
| Common Name         | Social Solidarity             |
| Type                | Internal Security Ministry    |
| Headquarters        | Citadel of Social Harmony     |
| Location            | Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne        |
| Employees           | Approx. 18 Million            |
| Status              | Active                        |
| Reporting Authority | Supreme President Leo Duprey  |

## Overview

The Ministry of Social Solidarity is the primary internal security, social administration, and compliance organization of Jardin.

## Session 1 Role

In [[Session 1 - Official Business]], [[Elena Verona]], a Social Solidarity commissar, arrived after the [[Mercier Assassination]] and attempted to contain the scene with Home Guard personnel. The public narrative later presented Verona as a harmed witness against the player characters.

The transcript clarifies that on Jardin, Social Solidarity functions as the local secret police and internal security organization. [[SolSec]] is separate and handles terrorism and external threats.

## Open Questions

- Whether Verona knowingly participated in the frame-up or was herself responding to manipulated information is not confirmed.
- The source of the "kill them all" warning/order received by Emma remains unresolved.

To foreign observers it resembles a mixture of:

* Internal Security Service
* Immigration Bureau
* Social Services Agency
* Political Police
* Population Management Ministry

To most citizens of Jardin, however, Social Solidarity is simply part of daily life.

Its officers assist with:

* Citizenship updates
* Housing assignments
* Reproductive approvals
* Family placement programs
* Employment disputes
* Community mediation
* Social stability initiatives

The Ministry maintains the largest citizen records network on the planet outside the Hall of Chosen Futures.

Every citizen encounters Social Solidarity repeatedly throughout their life.

Most never consider this unusual.

## Mission

Officially:

> "To ensure the continued harmony, prosperity, and stability of the people of Jardin through guidance, protection, and responsible stewardship."

Unofficially:

Social Solidarity exists to ensure the Perfection System continues to function.

If the Hall of Chosen Futures determines what society should become, Social Solidarity ensures society remains on that path.

## History

Social Solidarity emerged following the installation of the Solomani Party government after Jardin's defeat by the Solomani Autonomous Region.

Originally intended as a conventional internal security agency, its role expanded dramatically during the rise of the Perfectionist governments.

As family structures were gradually replaced by state-managed social planning systems, Social Solidarity absorbed responsibilities previously handled by:

* Families
* Religious institutions
* Local governments
* Community organizations

By 900 the Ministry had become one of the most powerful institutions on the planet.

Today it is widely considered the second most powerful organization on Jardin after the Supreme Presidency itself.

---

# Organizational Structure

## Directorate of Civic Harmony

The public face of the Ministry.

Responsible for:

* Community mediation
* Social assistance
* Housing concerns
* Employment disputes
* Citizen outreach

Most citizens encounter Civic Harmony officers regularly.

These officers wear light blue uniforms and are generally well-liked.

### Public Reputation

Helpful.

Professional.

Mildly intrusive.

---

## Directorate of Population Stewardship

Responsible for administration of:

* Reproductive approvals
* Pairing assignments
* Genetic compliance
* Child placement
* Family status records

Works closely with:

* Hall of Chosen Futures
* Ministry of Genetics

Among citizens this division is often referred to as:

> "The Matchmakers"

usually with varying degrees of affection.

### Public Reputation

Necessary.

Unavoidable.

Sometimes feared.

---

## Directorate of Social Stability

The Ministry's investigative branch.

Responsible for:

* Political extremism
* Organized dissent
* Subversive movements
* Social unrest
* Counter-perfectionist activity

Often mistaken for SolSec by outsiders.

The Directorate does little to correct this misunderstanding.

### Public Reputation

Polite.

Professional.

Dangerous.

---

## Directorate of Citizen Integrity

Maintains identity and compliance systems.

Responsible for:

* Identity verification
* Registry coordination
* Residency monitoring
* Immigration control
* Travel oversight

Works closely with:

* Port Sainte-Jeanne
* Hall of Chosen Futures
* Transstar

Many Syndicate investigations originate here.

### Public Reputation

Bureaucratic.

Efficient.

Impossible to avoid.

---

## Directorate of Overseas Affairs

Responsible for Jardinese citizens living off-world.

Monitors:

* Expatriate communities
* Returning citizens
* Foreign influence
* Counter-perfectionist ideology

This division maintains offices throughout Confederation space.

It is often the first point of contact between Social Solidarity and SolSec.

### Public Reputation

Almost invisible.

---

## Directorate of Protective Operations

The Ministry's tactical branch.

Handles:

* High-risk arrests
* Terrorism response
* VIP protection
* Riot suppression
* Strategic security operations

Members are heavily trained and equipped.

Fortunately, most citizens never encounter them.

### Public Reputation

Trouble has already begun if they arrive.

---

# Special Divisions

## Office of Social Forecasting

Analyzes long-term social trends.

Produces reports predicting:

* Crime
* Migration
* Political instability
* Economic stress

Many of its models are considered state secrets.

Works closely with the Hall of Chosen Futures.

---

## Office of Exceptional Citizens

Officially responsible for unusual citizen cases.

Responsibilities include:

* Exceptional aptitude assessments
* Genetic anomalies
* High-value talent identification

The office's true purpose remains classified.

Rumors persist that it recruits individuals possessing unusual cognitive abilities.

---

## Office of Historical Continuity

Preserves ideological consistency across generations.

Maintains:

* Educational standards
* Historical narratives
* Public memory projects

Often involved in disputes concerning controversial historical events.

---

# Relationship with SolSec

The relationship between Social Solidarity and SolSec is often described as:

> Cooperative rivalry.

Both organizations share information.

Both investigate threats.

Both maintain intelligence capabilities.

Neither entirely trusts the other.

---

## Areas of SolSec Authority

SolSec retains jurisdiction over:

* Confederation security
* Foreign espionage
* Treason
* Military intelligence
* Interstellar threats

---

## Areas of Social Solidarity Authority

Social Solidarity controls:

* Citizen compliance
* Social planning
* Internal stability
* Population management
* Political reliability
* Immigration

---

## Common Friction Points

### Jurisdiction

Social Solidarity often believes SolSec interferes in local matters.

SolSec often believes Social Solidarity conceals information.

### Political Priorities

SolSec serves the Confederation.

Social Solidarity serves Jardin.

These goals do not always align.

### Information Control

Both organizations possess enormous databases.

Both are reluctant to share everything.

---

## Typical Interaction

A SolSec agent arriving on Jardin receives full cooperation.

Provided:

* Proper credentials are presented.
* Local authority is respected.
* Appropriate notifications are filed.

Failure to follow these procedures frequently results in bureaucratic obstacles appearing with remarkable speed.

---

# Facilities

## Citadel of Social Harmony

Central headquarters.

Located within Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

A massive administrative complex combining:

* Offices
* Archives
* Command centers
* Training facilities

---

## Regional Harmony Centers

Found throughout Jardin.

Provide most public services.

---

## Citizen Assessment Offices

Located in every major population center.

Handle:

* Compliance reviews
* Registry updates
* Pairing administration

---

# Notable Leaders

## Supreme President Leo Duprey

Former Director-General of Social Solidarity.

Many observers believe he still exercises considerable influence within the Ministry.

---

## Director-General Camille Fournier

Current head of the Ministry.

A career administrator and loyal supporter of the Perfection System.

---

## Inspector-General Marc Villeneuve

Commander of the Directorate of Social Stability.

Widely feared by dissident organizations.

---

# Public Perception

Most citizens support Social Solidarity.

Many view the Ministry as:

* Helpful
* Necessary
* Protective

Critics argue that it has become too powerful.

Such criticisms are usually expressed quietly.

---

# Rivals

* Jardin Syndicate
* Sirius Rising
* Counter-Perfectionist Networks
* Foreign Intelligence Services

---

# Adventure Hooks

### The Missing Assessment

A citizen's entire life record disappears from the system.

### The Returning Traveller

A citizen returning from Imperial space is flagged for ideological review.

### Syndicate Infiltration

Evidence suggests a senior official is cooperating with Marcel Delphine.

### SolSec Dispute

Conflicting investigations place SolSec agents and Social Solidarity officers at odds.

### The Exceptional Citizen

A routine assessment reveals abilities no one can adequately explain.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

Social Solidarity should not be portrayed as cartoon villains.

Most employees genuinely believe they are helping society.

Many officers are compassionate.

Many are idealists.

Some are dangerous.

The Ministry's power comes not from fear alone but from legitimacy.

Most citizens cooperate because they believe the system works.

That belief is the true foundation of Social Solidarity's authority.

The Ministry's greatest weakness is that every aspect of Jardin depends upon it.

If Social Solidarity ever fails, the entire Perfection System may fail with it.




Secret agenda, debts, and leverage.
:::

title: Sunken Ship-Boat
summary: Possible submerged escape asset that may require a boat or submersible to reach and repair.
text:
# Sunken Ship-Boat

## Overview

One of Cass Pien's smuggling contacts reported a broken ship-boat sunk under the water. If the group can acquire a naval vessel, boat, or submersible and reach it, they may be able to repair it and use it as part of an escape route.

## Session 1 Context

At the end of [[Session 1 - Official Business]], the group identified this as one of several possible paths forward:

- Acquire a boat or submersible.
- Reach the sunken craft.
- Repair it.
- Use it to move away from direct Jardin controls or set up a later off-world escape.

## Limits

- The ship-boat's condition is unknown.
- The group does not yet have the needed naval transport.
- It is not confirmed that the ship-boat can leave the planet or only help them reach another departure route.

title: Timeline
summary: Chronological campaign timeline for Sparks of the Past.
text:
# Timeline

## 143-1105 - Official Business

### Summons to the Hall

The player characters are summoned to the [[Hall des Futurs Choisis]] in Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne for official genetic, military, financial, or AI-status review. Their military IDs allow many of them to enter armed, but the grouping is suspiciously convenient for a later frame-up.

### Mercier Broadcast

A news item reports that [[Alain Mercier]], Minister of Genetics, plans to reveal corruption in the genetic-affairs files at the coming Party Congress.

### Mercier Assassination

Mercier rushes through the office area and is killed by multiple shots. Several characters detect signs that their own weapons have moved or fired without normal handling. The group realizes they may have been framed.

### Social Solidarity Response

[[Elena Verona]] and [[Home Guard]] personnel attempt to contain the room. Emma receives a warning through her attached computer system that the authorities intend to kill the group. The confrontation turns into a firefight.

### Escape From the Hall

The group defeats the immediate security force, loots key equipment, and escapes when [[0-31 Saichi Maru (Annette)]] kills the pilot of an attacking walker/mech. The group rides the walker down the outside of the building and is collected by a driverless grav speeder.

### Marcel Delphine

The grav speeder carries the group through hidden service routes beneath [[Arcosphere Rousseau]] to [[Marcel Delphine]]. Marcel gives them [[Identity Veils]], explains the [[Jardin Syndicate]]'s interest, and claims the real corruption Mercier found came "from above."

### Aftermath

The official story becomes the [[Mercier Frame-Up]]. News broadcasts falsely blame genetic outcasts and show fabricated footage of [[Zoy (Damon)]] throwing a grenade. The group regroups in Emma's safe warehouse, gathers gear, contacts allies, and considers escape or investigation options.

### Open Paths

- Investigate who silenced Mercier.
- Obtain the Syndicate's unedited footage.
- Unlock [[Mercier's Locked Pad]].
- Reach the [[Transstar Sector Exchange]].
- Acquire forged identities.
- Find a boat/submersible and repair the [[Sunken Ship-Boat]].
- Move within the Jardin system before attempting off-world escape to [[Miasma]] or elsewhere.

## 143-1105 - Official Business

### Summons to the Hall

The player characters are summoned to the [[Hall des Futurs Choisis]] in Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne for official genetic, military, financial, or AI-status review. Their military IDs allow many of them to enter armed, but the grouping is suspiciously convenient for a later frame-up.

title: Transstar Sector Exchange
text:
# Secteur d'Échange Transstar
# Transstar Sector Exchange

*"The Market Never Sleeps."*

**Location:** Grand Axis District, Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne, Jardin

**Type:** Special Economic Zone

**Administrative Authority:** Transstar Economic Directorate

**Status:** Semi-Autonomous Economic District

**Population:** 2.1 Million Permanent Residents

**Daily Population:** 12-15 Million

**Primary Functions:**
- Interstellar Trade
- Banking
- Freight Brokerage
- Corporate Headquarters
- Commodity Markets
- Foreign Investment
- Arbitration Courts

**Nickname:** The Exchange

---

# Overview

The **Transstar Sector Exchange** is the economic heart of Jardin and one of the most important financial centers in the Solomani Front.

Located at the southern end of the Grand Axis between Rousseau Arcology and Port Sainte-Jeanne, the district serves as the central hub for commerce, banking, shipping contracts, insurance markets, and interstellar investment throughout the subsector.

Unlike most of Jardin, the Exchange operates under a special legal charter negotiated between Transstar, the Supreme Presidency, and the Solomani Party.

The result is a district that feels markedly different from the rest of the world.

Visitors often compare it to:

- A corporate city-state
- A permanent trade conference
- A civilized battlefield

The district is simultaneously one of the wealthiest and least ideological locations on Jardin.

Money speaks every language.

---

# History

## Foundation

The Exchange was established shortly after Jardin became the dominant economic power of the subsector.

As trade expanded, government officials recognized that traditional Party bureaucracy could not process commercial activity quickly enough.

Special exemptions were granted.

Corporate courts were established.

Foreign companies were invited.

Certain regulations were relaxed.

The experiment proved wildly successful.

---

## The Great Expansion

Following the Solomani Rim War, the Exchange became one of the Confederation's most important frontier trade hubs.

Merchants operating between:

- Confederation Worlds
- Imperial Border Systems
- Hive Federation Markets
- Frontier Colonies

all found themselves passing through Jardin.

Transstar aggressively expanded the district.

Within decades it became the largest concentration of commercial activity in the entire subsector.

---

# Governance

The Exchange exists under a special charter.

It is not independent.

But it is not fully governed by standard Jardinese law.

The arrangement remains controversial.

Officially:

- The Supreme Presidency governs the district.
- Confederation law applies.
- Social Solidarity retains authority.

In practice:

Transstar runs most day-to-day operations.

---

# The Exchange Authority

The district is administered by the **Exchange Authority**, a joint body consisting of:

- Transstar Executives
- Party Representatives
- Economic Ministry Officials
- Selected Corporate Delegates

Most decisions are made through negotiation.

---

# Exchange Security Bureau

## Corporate Police

The district's police force.

Often called:

> "Blue Jackets"

The Bureau handles:

- Financial crimes
- Commercial disputes
- Smuggling investigations
- Contract enforcement
- Corporate security

Uniforms are dark blue with silver trim.

Their authority exists only within Exchange territory.

Outside the district they possess no special privileges.

Inside the district they are feared.

---

# Relationship with Social Solidarity

The relationship is tense.

Social Solidarity considers the Exchange dangerously independent.

The Exchange Authority considers Social Solidarity dangerously intrusive.

Both cooperate when necessary.

Neither trusts the other.

---

# Relationship with SolSec

SolSec maintains a permanent office inside the Exchange.

Officially:

Counter-espionage.

Unofficially:

Everyone knows the district is crawling with intelligence services.

---

# Architecture

The district combines:

- Corporate Arcologies
- Financial Towers
- Luxury Hotels
- Trade Centers
- Elevated Transit Systems

The skyline differs dramatically from the monumental government architecture found elsewhere in Nouvelle Sainte-Jeanne.

Corporate logos are everywhere.

Many buildings are illuminated twenty-four hours a day.

The Exchange never truly sleeps.

---

# Major Districts

## The Grand Market

The commercial center.

Contains:

- Commodity exchanges
- Trade houses
- Shipping brokers
- Merchant guild offices

Thousands of contracts worth billions of credits are signed here daily.

---

## Transstar Plaza

The symbolic center of the district.

Dominated by the massive **Transstar Solomani Front Headquarters**.

The building houses:

- Regional Management
- Route Development Offices
- Freight Coordination Centers
- Political Liaison Offices

Many citizens consider it the true center of power in the subsector.

---

## Meridian Financial Quarter

Home to:

- Banks
- Investment Houses
- Insurance Syndicates
- Commodity Funds

The wealthiest square kilometer on Jardin.

---

## International Concourse

The most cosmopolitan district on the planet.

Visitors include:

- Imperial merchants
- Hive traders
- Aslan brokers
- Independent captains

The district feels noticeably less ideological than the rest of Jardin.

Business comes first.

---

## Broker's Row

Hundreds of freight brokers compete for contracts.

Deals ranging from:

- Single cargo containers
- Entire colony shipments
- Naval procurement contracts

change hands here.

---

# Commerce

Nearly forty percent of all legal interstellar commerce entering Jardin passes through the Exchange.

Major industries include:

- Shipping
- Finance
- Insurance
- Logistics
- Data Analysis
- Commodity Trading

The district generates enormous tax revenue.

Many Party officials privately dislike the Exchange.

Few are willing to damage something so profitable.

---

# Foreign Presence

Special permits allow limited foreign corporate representation.

Offices from:

- Imperial Trading Houses
- Hive Federation Companies
- Independent Merchant Leagues

can be found throughout the district.

The Exchange remains one of the few places on Jardin where foreigners are common.

---

# Criminal Activity

Money attracts crime.

The Exchange hosts:

- Corporate espionage
- Insider trading
- Data theft
- Smuggling
- Financial fraud

Most activity remains hidden beneath layers of legality.

---

# The Jardin Syndicate

The Syndicate maintains extensive influence throughout the Exchange.

Activities include:

- Money laundering
- False cargo manifests
- Identity creation
- Smuggling services

Many Exchange Security officers spend entire careers fighting Syndicate operations.

Most achieve only temporary victories.

---

# Notable Individuals

## Antoine Mercier

**Executive Director, Solomani Front Operations**

The most powerful Transstar executive on Jardin.

Frequently rumored to possess more influence than several planetary ministers.

---

## Isabelle Fournier

**Chief Commissioner, Exchange Security Bureau**

Respected, feared, and incorruptible.

At least according to official statements.

---

## Lucien Voss

**Senior Freight Broker**

A legend within the district.

Capable of arranging transport to almost anywhere.

For a price.

---

## Mara Kovalenko

**Director of Foreign Trade Relations**

Responsible for managing interactions with Imperial and Hive commercial interests.

Possesses one of the largest diplomatic contact networks in the subsector.

---

# Public Reputation

Citizens of Jardin often hold mixed opinions regarding the Exchange.

Supporters call it:

> The Engine of Prosperity

Critics call it:

> The Capitalist Exception

Most simply call it:

> Where the Money Is.

---

# Rumors

## The Hidden Market

An entire black-market exchange allegedly exists beneath the district.

---

## The Ghost Accounts

Anonymous investment funds appear to move billions of credits without identifiable owners.

---

## The Syndicate Ledger

A document exists proving corruption at the highest levels of the Exchange Authority.

Many have searched for it.

Few continue searching.

---

## The Foreign Vault

One of the banks allegedly stores data belonging to multiple intelligence agencies.

---

# Adventure Hooks

### Market Crash

Someone manipulates commodity markets, threatening the economy of multiple worlds.

### Missing Broker

A freight broker disappears after arranging a mysterious shipment.

### Corporate War

Two major corporations begin a covert conflict within the district.

### Syndicate Money

The Travellers uncover evidence of a massive laundering operation.

### SolSec Investigation

An intelligence operation threatens to expose corruption reaching into the Supreme Presidency itself.

### The Sealed Vault

A forgotten data vault from the Solomani Rim War is discovered beneath Transstar Plaza.

Everyone wants what is inside.

## Session 1 - Potential Refuge

Emma's smuggler contact identified the Transstar Sector Exchange as a possible refuge after the [[Mercier Frame-Up]]. The Exchange is described as an independent legal entity inside Jardin: not fully outside local reality, but separate enough that the group may not be direct enemies of the state while inside it.

The contact compared it to a corporate enclave or embassy-like safe zone. If the [[Identity Veils]] begin to fail, reaching the Exchange may allow the group to speak more openly with contacts and seek corporate or smuggling options.

---
:::gm
# Referee Notes

The Transstar Sector Exchange should feel fundamentally different from the rest of Jardin.

The Palace of Continuity is about power.

The Hall of Chosen Futures is about control.

The Closed Gardens are about science.

Rousseau is about people.

The Exchange is about money.

Here ideology bends around profit.

Party officials, merchants, smugglers, bankers, intelligence agents, and foreign traders all operate in close proximity.

It is the one place on Jardin where a person can genuinely believe that credits matter more than politics.

Whether that belief is true remains open to debate.


GM-only truth goes here.
:::
